nodes.name: Nox Swordstress Armor
nodes.snippet: Armor worn by swordstresses of the Eternal City. These women are the personal guards of the nightmaidens, and wear silk capes. Long ago, the Nox invoked the ire of the Greater Will, and were banished deep underground. Now they live under a
nodes.lore: Armor worn by swordstresses of the Eternal City. These women are the personal guards of the nightmaidens, and wear silk capes. Long ago, the Nox invoked the ire of the Greater Will, and were banished deep underground. Now they live under a false night sky, in eternal anticipation of their liege. Of the coming age of the stars. And their Lord of Night.

nodes.name: Omen Armor
nodes.snippet: Malformed armor resembling an Omen with its horns cut off.
nodes.lore: Malformed armor resembling an Omen with its horns cut off.

nodes.name: Official's Attire
nodes.snippet: Grubby blue robe worn by magisterial officials to carry out their grim tasks. Surveillance, executions, gruesome rituals... The darkest duties drive the wheels of mankind.
nodes.lore: Grubby blue robe worn by magisterial officials to carry out their grim tasks. Surveillance, executions, gruesome rituals... The darkest duties drive the wheels of mankind.

nodes.name: Page Garb
nodes.snippet: Garb worn by pages who serve the nobility and are mindful to keep them out of harm's way.
nodes.lore: Garb worn by pages who serve the nobility and are mindful to keep them out of harm's way.

nodes.snippet: Garb of a traveling perfumer of no renown.
nodes.lore: Garb of a traveling perfumer of no renown.

nodes.snippet: Robe worn by the Omenkillers, butchers of twisted conscience.
nodes.lore: Robe worn by the Omenkillers, butchers of twisted conscience.

nodes.name: Old Aristocrat Gown
nodes.snippet: Gown of soft cloth adorned with fine gold embroidery. Travel attire worn by nobles in the capital. Garb favored by the aged. Abandoning their birthplace after the Shattering, these undead wanderers are the pitiful product of unending life.
nodes.lore: Gown of soft cloth adorned with fine gold embroidery. Travel attire worn by nobles in the capital. Garb favored by the aged. Abandoning their birthplace after the Shattering, these undead wanderers are the pitiful product of unending life.

nodes.snippet: Lighter version of the Preceptor's Long Gown.
nodes.lore: Lighter version of the Preceptor's Long Gown.

nodes.name: Preceptor's Long Gown
nodes.snippet: A long, bright blue gown with the movements of the stars drawn upon it. Worn by the magic preceptors who served the Carian royals. Glintstone sorcerers are the descendants of astrologers, a fact that the Carians remain aware of. Even if the
nodes.lore: A long, bright blue gown with the movements of the stars drawn upon it. Worn by the magic preceptors who served the Carian royals. Glintstone sorcerers are the descendants of astrologers, a fact that the Carians remain aware of. Even if their fate has been long severed from the stars.

nodes.name: Prophet Robe (altered)
nodes.snippet: Lighter version of the Prophet Robe.
nodes.lore: Lighter version of the Prophet Robe.

nodes.name: Queen's Robe
nodes.snippet: Robe indicating the highest order of sorcerer. Worn by Rennala, Queen of the Full Moon. When Rennala, head of both the Academy of Raya Lucaria and the Carian royal family, lost her husband Radagon, her heart went along with him. And then, t
nodes.lore: Robe indicating the highest order of sorcerer. Worn by Rennala, Queen of the Full Moon. When Rennala, head of both the Academy of Raya Lucaria and the Carian royal family, lost her husband Radagon, her heart went along with him. And then, those at the academy realized. That Rennala was no champion, after all.

nodes.name: Prophet Robe
nodes.snippet: Robe of exiled prophets who foresaw that their faith would end in flames — and were persecuted and driven from their homes as a result. Its fabric scrapes against their skin like a file, and the rickety cart wheel around the neck warns pass
nodes.lore: Robe of exiled prophets who foresaw that their faith would end in flames — and were persecuted and driven from their homes as a result. Its fabric scrapes against their skin like a file, and the rickety cart wheel around the neck warns passers-by not to lend an ear to their meandering sermons.

nodes.name: Prisoner Clothing
nodes.snippet: Tattered clothing. Worn by a prisoner convicted of an appalling crime. Never once washed, every fiber is filthy.
nodes.lore: Tattered clothing. Worn by a prisoner convicted of an appalling crime. Never once washed, every fiber is filthy.

nodes.name: Ragged Armor
nodes.snippet: Armor pieced together from scavenged metal.
nodes.lore: Armor pieced together from scavenged metal.

nodes.snippet: Lighter version of the Ragged Armor.
nodes.lore: Lighter version of the Ragged Armor.

nodes.name: Radahn's Lion Armor (altered)
nodes.snippet: Lighter version of the Radahn's Lion Armor.
nodes.lore: Lighter version of the Radahn's Lion Armor.

nodes.name: Radahn's Lion Armor
nodes.snippet: Armor depicting the golden lion. Worn by General Radahn.
nodes.lore: Armor depicting the golden lion. Worn by General Radahn.

nodes.name: Raging Wolf Armor (altered)
nodes.snippet: Armor worn by Vargram the Raging Wolf, one of the first Tarnished to visit the Roundtable Hold.
nodes.lore: Armor worn by Vargram the Raging Wolf, one of the first Tarnished to visit the Roundtable Hold.

nodes.name: Raya Lucarian Robe
nodes.snippet: Robe worn by Raya Lucaria's magic scholars. Those who dedicate themselves to the study of glintstones formed from starry amber receive this modest yet elegant deep blue garb along with their vows of virtue and austerity. But with extended l
nodes.lore: Robe worn by Raya Lucaria's magic scholars. Those who dedicate themselves to the study of glintstones formed from starry amber receive this modest yet elegant deep blue garb along with their vows of virtue and austerity. But with extended life, one is apt to forget old vows.

nodes.name: Raging Wolf Armor
nodes.snippet: Armor worn by Vargram the Raging Wolf, one of the first Tarnished to visit the Roundtable Hold. According to the old legends, wolves are the shadows of the Empyrean. Vargram aspired to such a state himself.
nodes.lore: Armor worn by Vargram the Raging Wolf, one of the first Tarnished to visit the Roundtable Hold. According to the old legends, wolves are the shadows of the Empyrean. Vargram aspired to such a state himself.

nodes.name: Redmane Surcoat
nodes.snippet: Armor worn by soldiers who fought by General Radahn's side.
nodes.lore: Armor worn by soldiers who fought by General Radahn's side.

nodes.name: Ronin's Armor
nodes.snippet: Armor of the man who calls himself Shabriri. Highly functional and heavily worn.
nodes.lore: Armor of the man who calls himself Shabriri. Highly functional and heavily worn.

nodes.name: Rotten Gravekeeper Cloak
nodes.snippet: Thick, bristly cloak eaten through by scarlet rot. The symbol of an underground gravekeeper's station.
nodes.lore: Thick, bristly cloak eaten through by scarlet rot. The symbol of an underground gravekeeper's station.

nodes.snippet: Lighter version of the Redmane Knight Armor. The armor is not as sturdy as the original, but it still provides a decent amount of protection.

nodes.snippet: Armor worn by knights who fought by General Radahn's side.

nodes.name: Raptor's Black Feathers
nodes.snippet: Robe crafted with the black feathers of a bird of prey. Worn by the assassin Ravenmount.
nodes.lore: Robe crafted with the black feathers of a bird of prey. Worn by the assassin Ravenmount.

nodes.name: Royal Knight Armor
nodes.snippet: Silver armor of Loretta, a knight who served Miquella's Haligtree. Loretta, once a royal Carian knight, went on a journey in search of a haven for Albinaurics, and determined that the Haligtree was their best chance for eventual salvation.
nodes.lore: Silver armor of Loretta, a knight who served Miquella's Haligtree. Loretta, once a royal Carian knight, went on a journey in search of a haven for Albinaurics, and determined that the Haligtree was their best chance for eventual salvation.

nodes.snippet: Lighter version of the Royal Knight Armor. The armor is not as sturdy as the original, but it still provides a decent amount of protection.

nodes.lore: Lighter version of the Rotten Gravekeeper Cloak.

nodes.name: Royal Remains Armor
nodes.snippet: Armory graces with gold human bones. Worn by the unspeaking adherent of Sir Gideon the All-Knowing.
nodes.lore: Armory graces with gold human bones. Worn by the unspeaking adherent of Sir Gideon the All-Knowing.

nodes.name: Scaled Armor
nodes.snippet: Armor worn by Old Knight Istvan. The corroded metal is reinforced with rock-hard scales, making it high effective against non-physical attacks.
nodes.lore: Armor worn by Old Knight Istvan. The corroded metal is reinforced with rock-hard scales, making it high effective against non-physical attacks.

nodes.name: Sanguine Noble Robe
nodes.snippet: Robe of dyed black cloth featuring gold embroidery. Worn by the nobles who serve the Lord of Blood. The grand metallic pattern on the shoulder is a signifier of the noble rank they intend to claim upon the advent of the new dynasty they are
nodes.lore: Robe of dyed black cloth featuring gold embroidery. Worn by the nobles who serve the Lord of Blood. The grand metallic pattern on the shoulder is a signifier of the noble rank they intend to claim upon the advent of the new dynasty they are working to install.

nodes.name: Ruler's Robe
nodes.snippet: Luxurious robe worn by lords in a smaller nation. Its fine blue mantle serves to prove its wearer's status.
nodes.lore: Luxurious robe worn by lords in a smaller nation. Its fine blue mantle serves to prove its wearer's status.

nodes.name: Scaled Armor (altered)
nodes.snippet: Lighter version of the Scaled Armor
nodes.lore: Lighter version of the Scaled Armor

nodes.name: Sage Robe
nodes.snippet: Thick burgundy robe. Attire of the wise sages who were deemed heretical. Evidence that the wearer was driven from town.
nodes.lore: Thick burgundy robe. Attire of the wise sages who were deemed heretical. Evidence that the wearer was driven from town.

nodes.name: Scale Armor
nodes.snippet: Armor reinforced with small metal scales. The cape covering the shoulders is made with treated rock lizard skin, and provides ample protection against fire.
nodes.lore: Armor reinforced with small metal scales. The cape covering the shoulders is made with treated rock lizard skin, and provides ample protection against fire.

nodes.lore: Lighter version of the Traveling Maiden Robe

nodes.snippet: Lighter version of the Snow Witch Robe
nodes.lore: Lighter version of the Snow Witch Robe

nodes.name: Scarlet Tabard
nodes.snippet: Padded cloth armor with the telltale stains of the scarlet rot. Worn by foot soldiers in General Radahn's army

nodes.name: Shaman Furs
nodes.snippet: Raiment fashioned from a bristly pelt. Worn by ancestral follower shamans.
nodes.lore: Raiment fashioned from a bristly pelt. Worn by ancestral follower shamans.

nodes.name: Traveling Maiden Robe
nodes.snippet: Robe worn by traveling maidens. Maidens traversed the Lands Between for different reasons. Some to seek audience with the Fingers. Others, to find the Tarnished who they were destined to guide.
nodes.lore: Robe worn by traveling maidens. Maidens traversed the Lands Between for different reasons. Some to seek audience with the Fingers. Others, to find the Tarnished who they were destined to guide.

nodes.name: Snow Witch Robe
nodes.snippet: Witch's robe in the color of snow. Once worn by the snowy crone who the young Ranni encountered deep in the woods. She was a witch, and well versed in cold sorceries. It is said that the doll that houses Ranni's soul was modeled after her.
nodes.lore: Witch's robe in the color of snow. Once worn by the snowy crone who the young Ranni encountered deep in the woods. She was a witch, and well versed in cold sorceries. It is said that the doll that houses Ranni's soul was modeled after her. That old witch was Ranni's secret mentor.

nodes.name: Tree Sentinel Armor
nodes.snippet: Golden armor of the heavy cavalry Tree Sentinels who serve the Erdtree.
nodes.lore: Golden armor of the heavy cavalry Tree Sentinels who serve the Erdtree.

nodes.snippet: Lighter version of the Spellblade's Traveling Attire
nodes.lore: Lighter version of the Spellblade's Traveling Attire

nodes.name: Traveler's Clothes
nodes.snippet: Light yet sturdy clothes.
nodes.lore: Light yet sturdy clothes.

nodes.name: Spellblade's Traveling Attire
nodes.snippet: Glintstone sorcerer Rogier's traveling attire, graced with an intricate, aristocratic decoration. Strengthens glintstone sorcery skills.
nodes.lore: Glintstone sorcerer Rogier's traveling attire, graced with an intricate, aristocratic decoration. Strengthens glintstone sorcery skills.

nodes.lore: Lighter version of the Vagabond Knight Armor

nodes.name: Vagabond Knight Armor
nodes.snippet: Chest piece of a knight banished from their motherland. Dirty and battered after enduring a lengthy vagabond journey.
nodes.lore: Chest piece of a knight banished from their motherland. Dirty and battered after enduring a lengthy vagabond journey.

nodes.name: Veteran's Armor
nodes.snippet: Old armor that has seen many battles. Worn by Niall, the veteran general of Sol. Niall was a lone survivor who commanded spirits to defend his long-passed master. This aging general could not die, nor did he have anywhere to fade away.
nodes.lore: Old armor that has seen many battles. Worn by Niall, the veteran general of Sol. Niall was a lone survivor who commanded spirits to defend his long-passed master. This aging general could not die, nor did he have anywhere to fade away.

nodes.name: Twinned Armor
nodes.snippet: Armor depicting entwined twins of gold and silver.
nodes.lore: Armor depicting entwined twins of gold and silver.

nodes.name: Veteran's Armor (altered)
nodes.snippet: Lighter version of the Veteran's Armor
nodes.lore: Lighter version of the Veteran's Armor

nodes.name: Tree Surcoat
nodes.snippet: Blue surcoat draped over chainmail. The tree insignia emblazoned on the front depicts the Erdtree, and is a design beloved throughout the Lands Between.
nodes.lore: Blue surcoat draped over chainmail. The tree insignia emblazoned on the front depicts the Erdtree, and is a design beloved throughout the Lands Between.

nodes.name: Upper-class Robe
nodes.snippet: Embroidered, bright green robe worn by noble children. Donning such a robe is traditionally the child's first show of burgeoning independence.
nodes.lore: Embroidered, bright green robe worn by noble children. Donning such a robe is traditionally the child's first show of burgeoning independence.

nodes.name: Tree-and-beast Surcoat
nodes.snippet: Armor worn by soldiers loyal to Godrick the Grafted. The surcoat depicts the distant Erdtree and the beast regent, an emblem of the golden lineage. Both are symbols of glory now past.
nodes.lore: Armor worn by soldiers loyal to Godrick the Grafted. The surcoat depicts the distant Erdtree and the beast regent, an emblem of the golden lineage. Both are symbols of glory now past.

nodes.name: Vulgar Militia Armor
nodes.snippet: Armor worn by lean, mean, and filthy militiamen.
nodes.lore: Armor worn by lean, mean, and filthy militiamen.

nodes.name: White Reed Armor
nodes.snippet: Armor of the White Reeds, fashioned from banded iron plates. Worn by the Inabam head disciples of the great swordsman Okina.
nodes.lore: Armor of the White Reeds, fashioned from banded iron plates. Worn by the Inabam head disciples of the great swordsman Okina.

nodes.name: War Surgeon Gown
nodes.snippet: "Bloodstained white gown of the war surgeons who were effectively mercy killers. Of the surgeons that were abducted by the Lord of Blood none were able to tame the accursed blood. None but Varré, that is; though he was an exception."
nodes.lore: "Bloodstained white gown of the war surgeons who were effectively mercy killers. Of the surgeons that were abducted by the Lord of Blood none were able to tame the accursed blood. None but Varré, that is; though he was an exception."

nodes.name: War Surgeon Gown (altered)
nodes.snippet: Lighter version of the War Surgeon Gown.
nodes.lore: Lighter version of the War Surgeon Gown.

nodes.name: Azur's Manchettes
nodes.snippet: Manchettes corroded by blue-green glintstones. Worn by Azur, primeval current sorcerer.
nodes.lore: Manchettes corroded by blue-green glintstones. Worn by Azur, primeval current sorcerer.

nodes.name: Battlemage Manchettes
nodes.snippet: Manchettes worn by scholars of the Raya Lucaria Academy, tattered from being worn on the battlefield.
nodes.lore: Manchettes worn by scholars of the Raya Lucaria Academy, tattered from being worn on the battlefield.

nodes.name: Astrologer Gloves
nodes.snippet: Gloves fashioned from fine, supple cloth. Worn by those who look to the cosmos above. They read fate in the stars, and are said to be heirs of the glintstone sorcerers. But alas, the night sky no longer cradles fate.
nodes.lore: Gloves fashioned from fine, supple cloth. Worn by those who look to the cosmos above. They read fate in the stars, and are said to be heirs of the glintstone sorcerers. But alas, the night sky no longer cradles fate.

nodes.name: Bandit Manchettes
nodes.snippet: Manchettes made by simple wrapping dirty cloth around the arms. Worn by bandits.
nodes.lore: Manchettes made by simple wrapping dirty cloth around the arms. Worn by bandits.

nodes.name: Banished Knight Gauntlets
nodes.snippet: Thick, full set of armor covering the entire body. These gauntlets were worn by knights who, whether by misfortune or misdeed, were forced to abandon their homes. These fierce warriors were each and all accomplished. Perhaps that is why, de
nodes.lore: Thick, full set of armor covering the entire body. These gauntlets were worn by knights who, whether by misfortune or misdeed, were forced to abandon their homes. These fierce warriors were each and all accomplished. Perhaps that is why, despite their territorial losses, they were still named knights.

nodes.name: Zamor Armor
nodes.snippet: Armor worn by knights of Zamor, hailed as heroes in the War against the Giants. These long-lived warriors, clad in biting, freezing winds, are said to have been the mortal enemies of the Fire Giants since time immemorial.
nodes.lore: Armor worn by knights of Zamor, hailed as heroes in the War against the Giants. These long-lived warriors, clad in biting, freezing winds, are said to have been the mortal enemies of the Fire Giants since time immemorial.

nodes.name: Alberich's Bracers
nodes.snippet: Mad Tongue Alberich's bracers.
nodes.lore: Mad Tongue Alberich's bracers.

nodes.name: Beast Champion Gauntlets
nodes.snippet: Silver gauntlets engraved with tiny beasts. Worn by Bernahl the Recusant. Beasts are drawn to champions, and to lords. And this armor befits a champion worthy of becoming a lord. And that is what Bernahl was. Until his maiden threw herself
nodes.lore: Silver gauntlets engraved with tiny beasts. Worn by Bernahl the Recusant. Beasts are drawn to champions, and to lords. And this armor befits a champion worthy of becoming a lord. And that is what Bernahl was. Until his maiden threw herself into the fire.

nodes.name: Black Knife Gauntlets
nodes.snippet: Gauntlets used by the Black Knife Assassins. Crafted with scale armor that makes no sound. The assassins that carried out the deeds of the Night of the Black Knives were all women, and rumored to be Numen who had close ties with Marika hers
nodes.lore: Gauntlets used by the Black Knife Assassins. Crafted with scale armor that makes no sound. The assassins that carried out the deeds of the Night of the Black Knives were all women, and rumored to be Numen who had close ties with Marika herself.

nodes.name: Blackflame Monk Gauntlets
nodes.snippet: Black iron gauntlets featuring a flame motif. Attire of the Blackflame Monks.
nodes.lore: Black iron gauntlets featuring a flame motif. Attire of the Blackflame Monks.

nodes.name: Blaidd's Gauntlets
nodes.snippet: Well-worn black gauntlets of the man-wolf Blaidd.
nodes.lore: Well-worn black gauntlets of the man-wolf Blaidd.

nodes.name: Brave's Bracer
nodes.snippet: Gauntlets wrapped in thick leather. Garb worn by warriors of the frigid uplands. Has a fur lining which excels at keeping the cold at bay.
nodes.lore: Gauntlets wrapped in thick leather. Garb worn by warriors of the frigid uplands. Has a fur lining which excels at keeping the cold at bay.

nodes.name: Briar Gauntlets
nodes.snippet: Battered iron gauntlets from a foreign land. Worn by Elemer of the Briar.
nodes.lore: Battered iron gauntlets from a foreign land. Worn by Elemer of the Briar.

nodes.name: Bull-goat Gauntlets
nodes.snippet: Great Horned Tragoth's gauntlets. Covers its wearer with a pair of giant horns, providing staunch poise.
nodes.lore: Great Horned Tragoth's gauntlets. Covers its wearer with a pair of giant horns, providing staunch poise.

nodes.name: Bloodsoaked Manchettes
nodes.snippet: Manchettes formed from tightly-wound bandages soaked through with blood. Any protection is welcome for the festering arms beneath.
nodes.lore: Manchettes formed from tightly-wound bandages soaked through with blood. Any protection is welcome for the festering arms beneath.

nodes.name: Bloodhound Knight Gauntlets
nodes.snippet: Metal gauntlets of the Bloodhound Knights.
nodes.lore: Metal gauntlets of the Bloodhound Knights.

nodes.name: Blue Silver Bracelets
nodes.snippet: Bracelets crafted with blue silver. Worn by the wolf-riding Albinauric archers.
nodes.lore: Bracelets crafted with blue silver. Worn by the wolf-riding Albinauric archers.

nodes.name: Carian Knight Gauntlets
nodes.snippet: Gauntlets of the enchanted knights that once served the Carian royal family. The enchanted knights, anointed by the Lunar Queen, were heroes of the highest honors, but fell into disarray with the decline of the royal family.
nodes.lore: Gauntlets of the enchanted knights that once served the Carian royal family. The enchanted knights, anointed by the Lunar Queen, were heroes of the highest honors, but fell into disarray with the decline of the royal family.

nodes.name: Chain Gauntlets
nodes.snippet: Gauntlets made of woven chain. Standard issue for ordinary soldiers. Though plain in appearance, they offer dependable protection.
nodes.lore: Gauntlets made of woven chain. Standard issue for ordinary soldiers. Though plain in appearance, they offer dependable protection.

nodes.name: Cuckoo Knight Gauntlets
nodes.snippet: Gauntlets worn by Raya Lucaria Academy knights. Time has yet to dull their luster.
nodes.lore: Gauntlets worn by Raya Lucaria Academy knights. Time has yet to dull their luster.

nodes.name: Confessor Gloves
nodes.snippet: Black leather gloves of the church confessors. The churches outside the Lands Between, dedicated to the teachings of the Two Fingers, send confessors out to follow the guidance of grace. The confessors are loyal servants to the Two Fingers,
nodes.lore: Black leather gloves of the church confessors. The churches outside the Lands Between, dedicated to the teachings of the Two Fingers, send confessors out to follow the guidance of grace. The confessors are loyal servants to the Two Fingers, ready to hunt down and quietly dispose of their enemies.

nodes.name: Cleanrot Gauntlets
nodes.snippet: Gauntlets of the Cleanrot Knights, celebrated for their undefeated campaign in the Shattering. The Cleanrot Knights vowed to fight alongside Malenia, despite the inevitable, if gradual, putrefaction of their flesh. Their acceptance of their
nodes.lore: Gauntlets of the Cleanrot Knights, celebrated for their undefeated campaign in the Shattering. The Cleanrot Knights vowed to fight alongside Malenia, despite the inevitable, if gradual, putrefaction of their flesh. Their acceptance of their fate made these battles fiercest of all.

nodes.name: Champion Bracers
nodes.snippet: Bracers reserved for the bandlands' bravest. Proof that the wearer has slaughtered countless foes. Following the example of their chieftain Hoarah Loux, the brave warriors of the badlands shun excess adornment.
nodes.lore: Bracers reserved for the bandlands' bravest. Proof that the wearer has slaughtered countless foes. Following the example of their chieftain Hoarah Loux, the brave warriors of the badlands shun excess adornment.

nodes.name: Depraved Perfumer Gloves
nodes.snippet: Gloves worn by depraved perfumers.
nodes.lore: Gloves worn by depraved perfumers.

nodes.name: Champion Gauntlets
nodes.snippet: Gauntlets reserved for the badlands' bravest. Proof that the wearer has slaughtered countless foes. Following the example of their chieftain Hoarah Loux, the brave warriors of the badlands shun excess adornment.
nodes.lore: Gauntlets reserved for the badlands' bravest. Proof that the wearer has slaughtered countless foes. Following the example of their chieftain Hoarah Loux, the brave warriors of the badlands shun excess adornment.

nodes.name: Elden Lord Bracers
nodes.snippet: Bracers of Godfrey the first Elden Lord. The age of the Erdtree began amongst conflict, when Godfrey was lord of the battlefield. He led the War against the Giants. Faced the Storm Lord, alone. And then, there came a moment. When his last w
nodes.lore: Bracers of Godfrey the first Elden Lord. The age of the Erdtree began amongst conflict, when Godfrey was lord of the battlefield. He led the War against the Giants. Faced the Storm Lord, alone. And then, there came a moment. When his last worthy enemy fell. And it was then, as the story is told, that the hue of Lord Godfrey's eyes faded.

nodes.name: Eccentric's Manchettes
nodes.snippet: The knight Jerren's manchettes. Jerren preferred a nomadic existence, but after spending time as a guest of the Carian royals, he became the guest commander for General Radahn. And for the first time, the restless tumbleweed would be bound
nodes.lore: The knight Jerren's manchettes. Jerren preferred a nomadic existence, but after spending time as a guest of the Carian royals, he became the guest commander for General Radahn. And for the first time, the restless tumbleweed would be bound by honorable oath.

nodes.name: Drake Knight Gauntlets
nodes.snippet: Black iron gauntlets worn by drake knights. Features the spoils of a dragon catch as an emblem of pride as both dragon hunter and partaker of communion. From birth, drake knights speak not a word. They spend their lives pursuing the strengt
nodes.lore: Black iron gauntlets worn by drake knights. Features the spoils of a dragon catch as an emblem of pride as both dragon hunter and partaker of communion. From birth, drake knights speak not a word. They spend their lives pursuing the strength of dragons, for its sublime beauty and inspiration of awe

nodes.name: Crucible Gauntlets
nodes.snippet: Gauntlets of the Crucible Knights who served Godfrey, the first Elden Lord. Hold the power of the crucible of life, the primordial form of the Erdtree. Strengthen Aspects of the Crucible incantations. In time, the strength shown by these kn
nodes.lore: Gauntlets of the Crucible Knights who served Godfrey, the first Elden Lord. Hold the power of the crucible of life, the primordial form of the Erdtree. Strengthen Aspects of the Crucible incantations. In time, the strength shown by these knights, and even their appearance, was seen as chaotic and deserving of scorn.

nodes.name: Errant Sorcerer Manchettes
nodes.snippet: Commonplace leather manchettes. Their hands are inlaid with dulled glintstone.
nodes.lore: Commonplace leather manchettes. Their hands are inlaid with dulled glintstone.

nodes.name: Fire Monk Gauntlets
nodes.snippet: Black iron gauntlets featuring a flame motif. Attire of the Fire Monks.
nodes.lore: Black iron gauntlets featuring a flame motif. Attire of the Fire Monks.

nodes.name: Godrick Knight Gauntlets
nodes.snippet: Gauntlets worn by knights loyal to Godrick the Grafted. Time has yet to dull their luster.
nodes.lore: Gauntlets worn by knights loyal to Godrick the Grafted. Time has yet to dull their luster.

nodes.name: Gelmir Knight Gauntlets
nodes.snippet: Gauntlets worn by knights once loyal to Praetor Rykard. Time has yet to dull their luster.
nodes.lore: Gauntlets worn by knights once loyal to Praetor Rykard. Time has yet to dull their luster.

nodes.name: Foot Soldier Gauntlets
nodes.snippet: Metal gauntlets covered in nicks and dents. Widely issued to foot soldiers in a lord's army.
nodes.lore: Metal gauntlets covered in nicks and dents. Widely issued to foot soldiers in a lord's army.

nodes.name: Exile Gauntlets
nodes.snippet: Damaged iron gauntlets. Worn by soldiers sent to the penal colonies.
nodes.lore: Damaged iron gauntlets. Worn by soldiers sent to the penal colonies.

nodes.name: Fingerprint Gauntlets
nodes.snippet: Iron gauntlets singed and blistered by fingers. Worn by Vyke, knight of the Roundtable Hold. No other Tarnished was closer to the throne of the Elden Lord than Vyke. But without announcement, Vyke traveled far below the capital, and was sco
nodes.lore: Iron gauntlets singed and blistered by fingers. Worn by Vyke, knight of the Roundtable Hold. No other Tarnished was closer to the throne of the Elden Lord than Vyke. But without announcement, Vyke traveled far below the capital, and was scorched by the flame of frenzy. Did he make his choice for his maiden, or did some other force lure him with suggestion?

nodes.name: Godrick Soldier Gauntlets
nodes.snippet: Gauntlets worn by soldiers loyal to Godrick the Grafted. Rust-worn and stained through unending conflict.
nodes.lore: Gauntlets worn by soldiers loyal to Godrick the Grafted. Rust-worn and stained through unending conflict.

nodes.name: Fire Prelate Gauntlets
nodes.snippet: Black iron gauntlets with the appearance of flames. Attire of the Fire Prelates, commanders of the Fire Monks.
nodes.lore: Black iron gauntlets with the appearance of flames. Attire of the Fire Prelates, commanders of the Fire Monks.

nodes.name: Godskin Apostle Bracelets
nodes.snippet: Black bracelets of the Godskin Apostles.
nodes.lore: Black bracelets of the Godskin Apostles.

nodes.name: Highwayman Gauntlets
nodes.snippet: Hideously stained and dented gauntlets. Worn by former foot soldiers who have turned to banditry.
nodes.lore: Hideously stained and dented gauntlets. Worn by former foot soldiers who have turned to banditry.

nodes.name: Godskin Noble Bracelets
nodes.snippet: Black bracelets of the Godskin Nobles. Nobles are the most ancient apostles who are said to have assimilated inhuman physiology. Not unlike the crucible, the Erdtree in its primordial form.
nodes.lore: Black bracelets of the Godskin Nobles. Nobles are the most ancient apostles who are said to have assimilated inhuman physiology. Not unlike the crucible, the Erdtree in its primordial form.

nodes.snippet: Gauntlets worn by soldiers sworn to the Haligtree. Rust-worn and stained through unending conflict.
nodes.lore: Gauntlets worn by soldiers sworn to the Haligtree. Rust-worn and stained through unending conflict.

nodes.name: Haligtree Knight Gauntlets
nodes.snippet: Gauntlets worn by knights sworn to the Haligtree. Time has yet to dull their luster.
nodes.lore: Gauntlets worn by knights sworn to the Haligtree. Time has yet to dull their luster.

nodes.name: Hoslow's Gauntlets
nodes.snippet: Garish silver gauntlets decorated with red embellishments. Passed down through the Hoslow family.
nodes.lore: Garish silver gauntlets decorated with red embellishments. Passed down through the Hoslow family.

nodes.name: Kaiden Gauntlets
nodes.snippet: Gauntlets worn by Kaiden sellswords.
nodes.lore: Gauntlets worn by Kaiden sellswords.

nodes.name: Iron Gauntlets
nodes.snippet: Ordinary iron gauntlets.
nodes.lore: Ordinary iron gauntlets.

nodes.snippet: Bracelets made in the image of Erdtree branches. Minimal adornments made my Lord Goldmask's disciples. Those disciples have long since left him.
nodes.lore: Bracelets made in the image of Erdtree branches. Minimal adornments made my Lord Goldmask's disciples. Those disciples have long since left him.

nodes.name: Guardian Bracers
nodes.snippet: Engraved golden bracers. Worn by the guardians of the Minor Erdtree.
nodes.lore: Engraved golden bracers. Worn by the guardians of the Minor Erdtree.

nodes.name: Golden Prosthetic
nodes.snippet: Golden prosthetic forged using ancient techniques
nodes.lore: Golden prosthetic forged using ancient techniques

nodes.name: Knight Gauntlets
nodes.snippet: Iron gauntlets of an unknown knight. It is made of thin iron plate. Such metal armor is heavy, but provides considerable damage negation.
nodes.lore: Iron gauntlets of an unknown knight. It is made of thin iron plate. Such metal armor is heavy, but provides considerable damage negation.

nodes.name: Land Of Reeds Gauntlets
nodes.snippet: Gauntlets wrapped in tough leather. Worn by the warriors of the Land of Reeds
nodes.lore: Gauntlets wrapped in tough leather. Worn by the warriors of the Land of Reeds

nodes.name: Lusat's Manchettes
nodes.snippet: Manchettes corroded by blue glintstones. Worn by Lusat, primeval current sorcerer.
nodes.lore: Manchettes corroded by blue glintstones. Worn by Lusat, primeval current sorcerer.

nodes.name: Maliketh's Gauntlets
nodes.snippet: Beast's gauntlets made of black iron and decorated with gold. Worn by Maliketh the Black Blade. Maliketh, Queen Marika's loyral half-brother, bore a blade imbued with Destined Death, and there was not one demigod who did not fear him. Champ
nodes.lore: Beast's gauntlets made of black iron and decorated with gold. Worn by Maliketh the Black Blade. Maliketh, Queen Marika's loyral half-brother, bore a blade imbued with Destined Death, and there was not one demigod who did not fear him. Champions knew what was at stake. Indeed, that is what made them champions

nodes.name: Leyndell Soldier Gauntlets
nodes.snippet: Gauntlets worn by soldiers sworn to defend the royal capital of Leyndell. Rust-worn and stained through unending conflict.
nodes.lore: Gauntlets worn by soldiers sworn to defend the royal capital of Leyndell. Rust-worn and stained through unending conflict.

nodes.name: Leather Gloves
nodes.snippet: Lightweight and battle-proven leather gloves worn by a savvy soldier. Many admire the wearer of this armor for his chivalrous and forthright spirit.
nodes.lore: Lightweight and battle-proven leather gloves worn by a savvy soldier. Many admire the wearer of this armor for his chivalrous and forthright spirit.

nodes.name: Lionel's Gauntlets
nodes.snippet: Founded iron gauntlets.
nodes.lore: Founded iron gauntlets.

nodes.name: Malenia's Gauntlet
nodes.snippet: Gauntlet made of unalloyed gold. Worn by Malenia, Blade of Miquella. Malenia awaited Miquella at the foot of the husk. "My brother will keep his promise. He possesses the wisdom, the allure, of a god—he is the most fearsome Empyrean of all.
nodes.lore: Gauntlet made of unalloyed gold. Worn by Malenia, Blade of Miquella. Malenia awaited Miquella at the foot of the husk. "My brother will keep his promise. He possesses the wisdom, the allure, of a god—he is the most fearsome Empyrean of all."

nodes.name: Malformed Dragon Gauntlets
nodes.snippet: Malformed golden gauntlets.
nodes.lore: Malformed golden gauntlets.

nodes.name: Omen Gauntlets
nodes.snippet: Malformed Gauntlets resembling an Omen with its horns cut off.
nodes.lore: Malformed Gauntlets resembling an Omen with its horns cut off.

nodes.name: Mushroom Arms
nodes.snippet: Mushrooms found growing all over the body. These overgrown mushroom have colonized the arms.
nodes.lore: Mushrooms found growing all over the body. These overgrown mushroom have colonized the arms.

nodes.name: Mausoleum Knight Gauntlets
nodes.snippet: Gauntlets worn by headless knights who endlessly guard the Walking Mausoleum. Time has yet to dull their luster.
nodes.lore: Gauntlets worn by headless knights who endlessly guard the Walking Mausoleum. Time has yet to dull their luster.

nodes.name: Mausoleum Gauntlets
nodes.snippet: Gauntlets worn by headless soldiers who endlessly guard the Wandering Mausoleum. Rust-worn and stained through unending conflict.
nodes.lore: Gauntlets worn by headless soldiers who endlessly guard the Wandering Mausoleum. Rust-worn and stained through unending conflict.

nodes.name: Millicent's Gloves
nodes.snippet: Gloves worn by Millicent the Rotting
nodes.lore: Gloves worn by Millicent the Rotting

nodes.name: Nox Monk Bracelets
nodes.snippet: Bracelets worn by the monks of the Eternal City. Long ago, the Nox invoked the ire of the Greater Will, and were banished deep underground. Now they live under a false night sky, in eternal anticipation of their liege. Of the coming age of
nodes.lore: Bracelets worn by the monks of the Eternal City. Long ago, the Nox invoked the ire of the Greater Will, and were banished deep underground. Now they live under a false night sky, in eternal anticipation of their liege. Of the coming age of the stars. And their Lord of Night.

nodes.name: Noble's Gloves
nodes.snippet: Gloves of plush leather. Worn by expatriated royalty. A gift of such rare beauty, a royal wouldn't disgrace their illustrious heritage even if found dead, in a ditch, in the middle of nowhere, while wearing such splendid raiment.
nodes.lore: Gloves of plush leather. Worn by expatriated royalty. A gift of such rare beauty, a royal wouldn't disgrace their illustrious heritage even if found dead, in a ditch, in the middle of nowhere, while wearing such splendid raiment.

nodes.name: Perfumer Gloves
nodes.snippet: Gloves of an Erdtree Capital perfumer.
nodes.lore: Gloves of an Erdtree Capital perfumer.

nodes.name: Night's Cavalry Gauntlets
nodes.snippet: Pitch-black gauntlets thinly painted with dried blood. Worn by the Night's Cavalry who ride funeral steeds.
nodes.lore: Pitch-black gauntlets thinly painted with dried blood. Worn by the Night's Cavalry who ride funeral steeds.

nodes.name: Omenkiller Long Gloves
nodes.snippet: Long,stiff leather gloves worn by the Omenkillers.
nodes.lore: Long,stiff leather gloves worn by the Omenkillers.

nodes.name: Preceptor's Gloves
nodes.snippet: Trousers of the magic preceptors who serve the Carian royals. Black and perfectly discreet.
nodes.lore: Trousers of the magic preceptors who serve the Carian royals. Black and perfectly discreet.

nodes.name: Raya Lucarian Gauntlets
nodes.snippet: Gauntlets worn by Raya Lucaria Academy soldiers. Rust-worn and stained through unending conflict.
nodes.lore: Gauntlets worn by Raya Lucaria Academy soldiers. Rust-worn and stained through unending conflict.

nodes.snippet: Gauntlets pieced together from scavenged metal.
nodes.lore: Gauntlets pieced together from scavenged metal.

nodes.name: Radahn Soldier Gauntlets
nodes.snippet: Gauntlets worn by soldiers who fought by General Radahn's side. Rust-worn and stained through unending conflict.
nodes.lore: Gauntlets worn by soldiers who fought by General Radahn's side. Rust-worn and stained through unending conflict.

nodes.name: Radahn's Gauntlets
nodes.snippet: Gauntlets depicting the golden lion. Worn by General Radahn.
nodes.lore: Gauntlets depicting the golden lion. Worn by General Radahn.

nodes.name: Royal Knight Gauntlets
nodes.snippet: Silver gauntlets of Loretta, a knight who served Miquella's Haligtree. Loretta, once a royal Carian knight, went on a journey in search of a haven for Albinaurics, and determined that the Haligtree was their best chance for eventual salvati
nodes.lore: Silver gauntlets of Loretta, a knight who served Miquella's Haligtree. Loretta, once a royal Carian knight, went on a journey in search of a haven for Albinaurics, and determined that the Haligtree was their best chance for eventual salvation.

nodes.name: Ronin's Gauntlets
nodes.snippet: Gauntlets of the man who calls himself Shabriri. Highly functional and heavily worn.
nodes.lore: Gauntlets of the man who calls himself Shabriri. Highly functional and heavily worn.

nodes.name: Queen's Bracelets
nodes.snippet: Bracelets indicating the highest order of sorcerer. Worn by Rennala, Queen of the Full Moon. When Rennala, head of both the Academy of Raya Lucaria and the Carian royal family, lost her husband Radagon, her heart went along with him. And th
nodes.lore: Bracelets indicating the highest order of sorcerer. Worn by Rennala, Queen of the Full Moon. When Rennala, head of both the Academy of Raya Lucaria and the Carian royal family, lost her husband Radagon, her heart went along with him. And then, those at the academy realized. That Rennala was no champion, after all.

nodes.name: Royal Remains Gauntlets
nodes.snippet: Gauntlets graces with gold human bones. Worn by the unspeaking adherent of Sir Gideon the All-Knowing.
nodes.lore: Gauntlets graces with gold human bones. Worn by the unspeaking adherent of Sir Gideon the All-Knowing.

nodes.name: Traveler's Gloves
nodes.snippet: Gloves of a traveling perfumer of no renown.
nodes.lore: Gloves of a traveling perfumer of no renown.

nodes.name: Tree Sentinel Gauntlets
nodes.snippet: Golden gauntlets of the heavy cavalry Tree Sentinels who serve the Erdtree.
nodes.lore: Golden gauntlets of the heavy cavalry Tree Sentinels who serve the Erdtree.

nodes.name: Traveler's Manchettes
nodes.snippet: Light yet sturdy leather manchettes.
nodes.lore: Light yet sturdy leather manchettes.

nodes.name: Sorcerer Manchettes
nodes.snippet: Manchettes worn by Raya Lucaria's magic scholars. Made by wrapping a simple cloth around the forearms.
nodes.lore: Manchettes worn by Raya Lucaria's magic scholars. Made by wrapping a simple cloth around the forearms.

nodes.name: Twinned Gauntlets
nodes.snippet: Gauntlets depicting entwined twins of gold and silver.
nodes.lore: Gauntlets depicting entwined twins of gold and silver.

nodes.name: Traveling Maiden Gloves
nodes.snippet: Gloves worn by traveling maidens.
nodes.lore: Gloves worn by traveling maidens.

nodes.name: Vagabond Knight Gauntlets
nodes.snippet: Gauntlets of a knight banished form their motherland. Dirty and battered after enduring a lengthy vagabond journey. Metal armor is heavy, but also sturdy, offering significant damage negation.
nodes.lore: Gauntlets of a knight banished form their motherland. Dirty and battered after enduring a lengthy vagabond journey. Metal armor is heavy, but also sturdy, offering significant damage negation.

nodes.name: Veteran's Gauntlets
nodes.snippet: Old gauntlets that has seen many battles. Worn by Niall, the veteran general of Sol.
nodes.lore: Old gauntlets that has seen many battles. Worn by Niall, the veteran general of Sol.

nodes.name: Scaled Gauntlets
nodes.snippet: Gauntlets worn by Old Knight Istvan. The corroded metal is reinforced with rock-hard scales, making it high effective against non-physical attacks. Istvan is one of a few wizened Tarnished who survived to this day.
nodes.lore: Gauntlets worn by Old Knight Istvan. The corroded metal is reinforced with rock-hard scales, making it high effective against non-physical attacks. Istvan is one of a few wizened Tarnished who survived to this day.

nodes.name: Spellblade's Gloves
nodes.snippet: Glintstone sorcerer Rogier's traveling attire, graced with an intricate, aristocratic decoration. Strengthens glintstone sorcery skills.
nodes.lore: Glintstone sorcerer Rogier's traveling attire, graced with an intricate, aristocratic decoration. Strengthens glintstone sorcery skills.

nodes.name: Vulgar Militia Gauntlets
nodes.snippet: Gauntlets worn by lean, mean, and filthy militiamen.
nodes.lore: Gauntlets worn by lean, mean, and filthy militiamen.

nodes.name: Aristocrat Hat
nodes.snippet: Leather hat decorated with a withered feather. Travel attire worn by nobles in the capital, and the belonging of a family which claims a knight as an ancestor.
nodes.lore: Leather hat decorated with a withered feather. Travel attire worn by nobles in the capital, and the belonging of a family which claims a knight as an ancestor.

nodes.name: Warrior Gauntlets
nodes.snippet: Gauntlets of a nomadic warrior. Crafted from sturdy leather.
nodes.lore: Gauntlets of a nomadic warrior. Crafted from sturdy leather.

nodes.name: Aristocrat Headband
nodes.snippet: Valuable headband featuring fine goldwork.
nodes.lore: Valuable headband featuring fine goldwork.

nodes.name: Alberich's Pointed Hat (altered)
nodes.snippet: Lighter version of the Alberich's Pointed Hat.
nodes.lore: Lighter version of the Alberich's Pointed Hat.

nodes.name: Alberich's Pointed Hat
nodes.snippet: Mad Tongue Alberich's pointed hat, a sign of a heretical practitioner.
nodes.lore: Mad Tongue Alberich's pointed hat, a sign of a heretical practitioner.

nodes.name: War Surgeon Gloves
nodes.snippet: Bloodstained white gloves of the war surgeons who were effectively mercy killers. Of the surgeons that were abducted by the Lord of Blood none were able to tame the accursed blood. None but Varré, that is; though he was an exception
nodes.lore: Bloodstained white gloves of the war surgeons who were effectively mercy killers. Of the surgeons that were abducted by the Lord of Blood none were able to tame the accursed blood. None but Varré, that is; though he was an exception

nodes.name: Zamor Bracelets
nodes.snippet: Bracelets worn by knights of Zamor, hailed as heroes in the War against the Giants. These long-lived warriors, clad in biting, freezing winds, are said to have been the mortal enemies of the Fire Giants since time immemorial.
nodes.lore: Bracelets worn by knights of Zamor, hailed as heroes in the War against the Giants. These long-lived warriors, clad in biting, freezing winds, are said to have been the mortal enemies of the Fire Giants since time immemorial.

nodes.name: White Reed Gauntlets
nodes.snippet: Gauntlets of the White Reeds, fashioned from banded iron plates. Worn by the Inabam head disciples of the great swordsman Okina. The Inaba traveled in pursuit of not only the master who abandoned them, but also proper deaths, won honorably
nodes.lore: Gauntlets of the White Reeds, fashioned from banded iron plates. Worn by the Inabam head disciples of the great swordsman Okina. The Inaba traveled in pursuit of not only the master who abandoned them, but also proper deaths, won honorably through hard-fought combat. To be a White Reed is to seek death; fitting for the Inaba, who desired nothingmore than to die by the sword.

nodes.name: Bandit Mask
nodes.snippet: Mask of black cloth that covers the mouth. Favored by bandits for hiding their faces, it also provides some protection against poison.
nodes.lore: Mask of black cloth that covers the mouth. Favored by bandits for hiding their faces, it also provides some protection against poison.

nodes.name: Black Dumpling
nodes.snippet: Mask forced on a victim's head to lend torture an extra degree of cruelty. It magnifies one's fears and make them acutely aware of all forms of pain Raises attack power when the wearer suffers from madness. When the Black Dumpling goes on,
nodes.lore: Mask forced on a victim's head to lend torture an extra degree of cruelty. It magnifies one's fears and make them acutely aware of all forms of pain Raises attack power when the wearer suffers from madness. When the Black Dumpling goes on, the torturer no longer seeks answers; only to inflict suffering without hope of relief.

nodes.name: Banished Knight Helm (altered)
nodes.snippet: Lighter version of the Banished Knight Helm
nodes.lore: Lighter version of the Banished Knight Helm

nodes.name: Ash-of-war Scarab
nodes.snippet: Gray scarab worn directly on the head.
nodes.lore: Gray scarab worn directly on the head.

nodes.name: Astrologer Hood
nodes.snippet: Hood fashioned from supple cloth. Worn by those who look to the cosmos above. They read fate in the stars, and are said to be heirs of the glintstone sorcerers. But alas, the night sky no longer cradles fate.
nodes.lore: Hood fashioned from supple cloth. Worn by those who look to the cosmos above. They read fate in the stars, and are said to be heirs of the glintstone sorcerers. But alas, the night sky no longer cradles fate.

nodes.name: Black Hood
nodes.snippet: Hood of lusterless black fabric that conceals its wearer's identity.
nodes.lore: Hood of lusterless black fabric that conceals its wearer's identity.

nodes.name: Azur's Glintstone Crown
nodes.snippet: Crown of Azur, primeval current sorcerer, set with a prominent blue-green glintstone.
nodes.lore: Crown of Azur, primeval current sorcerer, set with a prominent blue-green glintstone.

nodes.name: Banished Knight Helm
nodes.snippet: Thick, full set of armor covering the entire body. This helm was worn by knights who, whether by misfortune or misdeed, were forced to abandon their homes. These fierce warriors were each and all accomplished. Perhaps that is why, despite t
nodes.lore: Thick, full set of armor covering the entire body. This helm was worn by knights who, whether by misfortune or misdeed, were forced to abandon their homes. These fierce warriors were each and all accomplished. Perhaps that is why, despite their territorial losses, they were still named knights.

nodes.name: Beast Champion Helm
nodes.snippet: Silver helm engraved with tiny beasts. Worn by Bernahl the Recusant.
nodes.lore: Silver helm engraved with tiny beasts. Worn by Bernahl the Recusant.

nodes.name: Black Wolf Mask
nodes.snippet: A mask fashioned after the head of a black wolf.
nodes.lore: A mask fashioned after the head of a black wolf.

nodes.name: Bloodsoaked Mask
nodes.snippet: Mask formed from tightly-wound bandages soaked through with blood. Even the most ghastly cover is more inviting than the festering face beneath.
nodes.lore: Mask formed from tightly-wound bandages soaked through with blood. Even the most ghastly cover is more inviting than the festering face beneath.

nodes.name: Black Knife Hood
nodes.snippet: Hood used by the Black Knife Assassins. The assassins that carried out the deeds of the Night of the Black Knives were all women, and rumored to be N u men who had close ties with Marika herself.
nodes.lore: Hood used by the Black Knife Assassins. The assassins that carried out the deeds of the Night of the Black Knives were all women, and rumored to be N u men who had close ties with Marika herself.

nodes.name: Blue Silver Mail Hood
nodes.snippet: Chainmail hood crafted with blue silver. Worn by the wolf-riding Albinauric archers.
nodes.lore: Chainmail hood crafted with blue silver. Worn by the wolf-riding Albinauric archers.

nodes.name: Bloodhound Knight Helm
nodes.snippet: Metal helm with a pointed beak. Worn by the Bloodhound Knights. The Bloodhound Knights are trained as hunters and known to be unshakable trackers. Without the use of language, each knight chooses his own master. Once the decision has been m
nodes.lore: Metal helm with a pointed beak. Worn by the Bloodhound Knights. The Bloodhound Knights are trained as hunters and known to be unshakable trackers. Without the use of language, each knight chooses his own master. Once the decision has been made, the knight stays loyal for life.

nodes.name: Blackguard's Iron Mask
nodes.snippet: Iron mask forced on convicted prisoners. Worn by the blackguard Big Boggart. To Boggart, the mask made him all the more threatening, and helped him get what he wanted. But it was also a mirror of his emptiness.
nodes.lore: Iron mask forced on convicted prisoners. Worn by the blackguard Big Boggart. To Boggart, the mask made him all the more threatening, and helped him get what he wanted. But it was also a mirror of his emptiness.

nodes.name: Blue Festive Hood
nodes.snippet: Hood worn by dancers at the festivities in Dominula, the village of windmills. Use of this hood, dyed in a stunning blue, is only permitted for the young maids who play the central role in the festival.
nodes.lore: Hood worn by dancers at the festivities in Dominula, the village of windmills. Use of this hood, dyed in a stunning blue, is only permitted for the young maids who play the central role in the festival.

nodes.name: Blackflame Monk Hood
nodes.snippet: Mail hood of iron links covering a headband engraved with black flames.
nodes.lore: Mail hood of iron links covering a headband engraved with black flames.

nodes.name: Blue Cloth Cowl
nodes.snippet: Cowl of a nomadic warrior. The blue color of its fabric symbolizes brisk waters, as fluid and flowing as the sword in the hand of its wearer. Just as still waters turn foul, stagnation leads to decay. Warriors must remain ever-drifting.
nodes.lore: Cowl of a nomadic warrior. The blue color of its fabric symbolizes brisk waters, as fluid and flowing as the sword in the hand of its wearer. Just as still waters turn foul, stagnation leads to decay. Warriors must remain ever-drifting.

nodes.name: Champion Headband
nodes.snippet: Headband reserved for the badlands' bravest. Proof that the wearer has slaughtered countless foes. Following the example of their chieftain Hoarah Loux, the brave warriors of the badlands shun excess adornment.
nodes.lore: Headband reserved for the badlands' bravest. Proof that the wearer has slaughtered countless foes. Following the example of their chieftain Hoarah Loux, the brave warriors of the badlands shun excess adornment.

nodes.name: Chain Coif
nodes.snippet: Coif made of woven chain. Standard issue for ordinary soldiers. Though plain in appearance, it offers dependable protection.
nodes.lore: Coif made of woven chain. Standard issue for ordinary soldiers. Though plain in appearance, it offers dependable protection.

nodes.name: Bull-goat Helm
nodes.snippet: Great Horned Tragoth's helm, adorned with a gold bull-goat motif. Provides high poise.
nodes.lore: Great Horned Tragoth's helm, adorned with a gold bull-goat motif. Provides high poise.

nodes.name: Brave's Cord Circlet
nodes.snippet: Warrior's headband of woven leather. Garb worn by warriors of the frigid uplands. The small flags, white cloths and sheaves of wheat often found hung from these headbands are prayers of farewell from the people of their home. In bidding far
nodes.lore: Warrior's headband of woven leather. Garb worn by warriors of the frigid uplands. The small flags, white cloths and sheaves of wheat often found hung from these headbands are prayers of farewell from the people of their home. In bidding farewell t warriors departing on a journey from which they aren't like to return, this small act offers only meager relief.

nodes.name: Cerulean Tear Scarab
nodes.snippet: Blue scarab worn directly on the head. These scarabs roll clumps of cerulean tears during their labors.
nodes.lore: Blue scarab worn directly on the head. These scarabs roll clumps of cerulean tears during their labors.

nodes.name: Cleanrot Helm
nodes.snippet: Winged helm of the Cleanrot Knights, celebrated for their undefeated campaign in the Shattering.
nodes.lore: Winged helm of the Cleanrot Knights, celebrated for their undefeated campaign in the Shattering.

nodes.name: Carian Knight Helm
nodes.snippet: Helm of the enchanted knights that once served the Carian royal family. The enchanted knights, anointed by the Lunar Queen, were heroes of the highest honors, but fell into disarray with the decline of the royal family.
nodes.lore: Helm of the enchanted knights that once served the Carian royal family. The enchanted knights, anointed by the Lunar Queen, were heroes of the highest honors, but fell into disarray with the decline of the royal family.

nodes.name: Briar Helm
nodes.snippet: Battered iron helm from a foreign land. Worn by Elemer of he Brair.
nodes.lore: Battered iron helm from a foreign land. Worn by Elemer of he Brair.

nodes.name: Brave's Leather Helm
nodes.snippet: Battlewear wrapped in thick leather. Garb worn by warriors of the frigid uplands.
nodes.lore: Battlewear wrapped in thick leather. Garb worn by warriors of the frigid uplands.

nodes.name: Cleanrot Helm (altered)
nodes.snippet: Lighter version of the Cleanrot Helm
nodes.lore: Lighter version of the Cleanrot Helm

nodes.name: Confessor Hood
nodes.snippet: Black hood for blending in with the darkness. Worn by church confessors. The churches outside the Lands Between, dedicated to the teachings of the Two Fingers, send confessors out to follow the guidance of grace. The confessors are loyal se
nodes.lore: Black hood for blending in with the darkness. Worn by church confessors. The churches outside the Lands Between, dedicated to the teachings of the Two Fingers, send confessors out to follow the guidance of grace. The confessors are loyal servants to the Two Fingers, ready to hunt down and quietly dispose of their enemies.

nodes.name: Commoner's Headband (altered)
nodes.snippet: Lighter version of the Commoner's Headband
nodes.lore: Lighter version of the Commoner's Headband

nodes.name: Confessor Hood (altered)
nodes.snippet: Lighter version of the Confessor Hood
nodes.lore: Lighter version of the Confessor Hood

nodes.name: Commoner's Headband
nodes.snippet: A headband with the cloth removed. Standard wear for commoners of the Lands Between.
nodes.lore: A headband with the cloth removed. Standard wear for commoners of the Lands Between.

nodes.name: Consort's Mask
nodes.snippet: Mask worn by Tanith, Lady of the Volcano Manor, in the image of a foreign queen.
nodes.lore: Mask worn by Tanith, Lady of the Volcano Manor, in the image of a foreign queen.

nodes.name: Crucible Axe Helm
nodes.snippet: Helm of the Crucible Knights who served Godfrey, the first Elden Lord. The axe ornamentation is the knight Ordovis's mark, displayed also by his men. Holds the power of the crucible of life, the primordial form of the Erdtree. Strengthens A
nodes.lore: Helm of the Crucible Knights who served Godfrey, the first Elden Lord. The axe ornamentation is the knight Ordovis's mark, displayed also by his men. Holds the power of the crucible of life, the primordial form of the Erdtree. Strengthens Aspects of the Crucible incantations.

nodes.name: Crimson Hood
nodes.snippet: A hooded cloak of vivid crimson. Worn by expatriates royalty. Increases vigor.
nodes.lore: A hooded cloak of vivid crimson. Worn by expatriates royalty. Increases vigor.

nodes.name: Crimson Tear Scarab
nodes.snippet: Red scarab worn directly on the head. The red scarabs roll clumps of crimson tears during their labors.
nodes.lore: Red scarab worn directly on the head. The red scarabs roll clumps of crimson tears during their labors.

nodes.name: Crucible Tree Helm
nodes.snippet: Helm of the Crucible Knights who served Godfrey, the first Elden Lord. The great tree ornamentation is the knight Siluria's mark, displayed also by her men. Holds the power of the crucible of life, the primordial form of the Erdtree. Streng
nodes.lore: Helm of the Crucible Knights who served Godfrey, the first Elden Lord. The great tree ornamentation is the knight Siluria's mark, displayed also by her men. Holds the power of the crucible of life, the primordial form of the Erdtree. Strengthens Aspects of the Crucible incantations.

nodes.name: Diallos's Mask
nodes.snippet: Replica of a twin-tailed silver helm with flowery adornments. Only, without the twin tails.
nodes.lore: Replica of a twin-tailed silver helm with flowery adornments. Only, without the twin tails.

nodes.name: Drake Knight Helm (altered)
nodes.snippet: Lighter version of the Drake Knight Helm.
nodes.lore: Lighter version of the Drake Knight Helm.

nodes.name: Depraved Perfumer Headscarf
nodes.snippet: Headscarf worn by depraved perfumers.
nodes.lore: Headscarf worn by depraved perfumers.

nodes.name: Cuckoo Knight Helm
nodes.snippet: Helm worn by Raya Lucaria Academy knights. Its brow is adorned with a single cuckoo's feather.
nodes.lore: Helm worn by Raya Lucaria Academy knights. Its brow is adorned with a single cuckoo's feather.

nodes.name: Duelist Helm
nodes.snippet: Bronze helm decorated with innumerable snakes. Worn by gladiators who were driven from the colosseum. The wearer becomes a slightly easier target for foes. The snake is viewed as a traitor to the Erdtree, and the audience delighted in seein
nodes.lore: Bronze helm decorated with innumerable snakes. Worn by gladiators who were driven from the colosseum. The wearer becomes a slightly easier target for foes. The snake is viewed as a traitor to the Erdtree, and the audience delighted in seeing these bronze effigies beaten and battered.

nodes.name: Eccentric's Hood
nodes.snippet: A pointy, ragged hood. Worn by the knight Jerren. The hood hides an aged, bearded mask. Jerren served General Radahn as a guest commander, and they are said to have sworn an oath of honorable death to one another.
nodes.lore: A pointy, ragged hood. Worn by the knight Jerren. The hood hides an aged, bearded mask. Jerren served General Radahn as a guest commander, and they are said to have sworn an oath of honorable death to one another.

nodes.name: Drake Knight Helm
nodes.snippet: Black iron helm worn by drake knights. Features the spoils of a dragon catch as an emblem of pride as both dragon hunter and partaker of communion. From birth, drake knights speak not a word. They spend their lives pursuing the strength of
nodes.lore: Black iron helm worn by drake knights. Features the spoils of a dragon catch as an emblem of pride as both dragon hunter and partaker of communion. From birth, drake knights speak not a word. They spend their lives pursuing the strength of dragons, for its sublime beauty and inspiration of awe

nodes.name: Eccentric's Hood (altered)
nodes.snippet: Lighter version of the Eccentric's Hood
nodes.lore: Lighter version of the Eccentric's Hood

nodes.name: Elden Lord Crown
nodes.snippet: Crown of Godfrey, the first Elden Lord. The age of the Erdtree began amongst conflict, when Godfrey was lord of the battlefield. He led the War against the Giants. Faced the Storm Lord, alone. And then, there came a moment. When his last wo
nodes.lore: Crown of Godfrey, the first Elden Lord. The age of the Erdtree began amongst conflict, when Godfrey was lord of the battlefield. He led the War against the Giants. Faced the Storm Lord, alone. And then, there came a moment. When his last worthy enemy fell. And it was then, as the story is told, that the hue of Lord Godfrey's eyes faded.

nodes.name: Envoy Crown
nodes.snippet: The soft bundle worn on the head by Oracle Envoys. Densely wrapped in several layers of cloth. Raises the potency of bubble-based skills. No one knows what the cloth hides, but some claim to have heard a faint whimpering from inside. It mus
nodes.lore: The soft bundle worn on the head by Oracle Envoys. Densely wrapped in several layers of cloth. Raises the potency of bubble-based skills. No one knows what the cloth hides, but some claim to have heard a faint whimpering from inside. It must have been their imagination.

nodes.name: Exile Hood
nodes.snippet: Deep red hood that covers the whole head. Worn by soldiers sent to the penal colonies, who were required to keep their faces hidden.
nodes.lore: Deep red hood that covers the whole head. Worn by soldiers sent to the penal colonies, who were required to keep their faces hidden.

nodes.name: Festive Hood (altered)
nodes.snippet: Lighter version of the Festive Hood
nodes.lore: Lighter version of the Festive Hood

nodes.name: Festive Hood
nodes.snippet: Hood worn by dancers at the festivities in Dominula, the village of windmills. Decorated with many-colored flowers.
nodes.lore: Hood worn by dancers at the festivities in Dominula, the village of windmills. Decorated with many-colored flowers.

nodes.name: Finger Maiden Fillet
nodes.snippet: Fillet worn by maidens who serve the Two Fingers. The maidens live to serve a chosen Tarnished, sharing their guidance and the wisdom of the Two Fingers. The guidance of grace would ensure that the pair be brought together. Or at least, suc
nodes.lore: Fillet worn by maidens who serve the Two Fingers. The maidens live to serve a chosen Tarnished, sharing their guidance and the wisdom of the Two Fingers. The guidance of grace would ensure that the pair be brought together. Or at least, such was the promise long ago.

nodes.name: Fire Monk Hood
nodes.snippet: Mail hood of iron links covering a headband engraved with flames. Attire of the Fire Monks.
nodes.lore: Mail hood of iron links covering a headband engraved with flames. Attire of the Fire Monks.

nodes.name: Fire Prelate Helm
nodes.snippet: Helm engraved with a sacred countenance and housing a cauldron. Attire of the Fire Prelates, commanders of the Fire Monks.
nodes.lore: Helm engraved with a sacred countenance and housing a cauldron. Attire of the Fire Prelates, commanders of the Fire Monks.

nodes.name: Fia's Hood
nodes.snippet: Hood of black cloth that covers the whole head. Worn by Fia, the Deathbed Companion, on her journey after being exiled from her home.
nodes.lore: Hood of black cloth that covers the whole head. Worn by Fia, the Deathbed Companion, on her journey after being exiled from her home.

nodes.name: Fingerprint Helm
nodes.snippet: Iron helm singed and blistered by fingers. Worn by Vyke, knight of the Roundtable Hold. No other Tarnished was closer to the throne of the Elden Lord than Vyke. But without announcement, Vyke traveled far below the capital, and was scorched
nodes.lore: Iron helm singed and blistered by fingers. Worn by Vyke, knight of the Roundtable Hold. No other Tarnished was closer to the throne of the Elden Lord than Vyke. But without announcement, Vyke traveled far below the capital, and was scorched by the flame of frenzy. Did he make his choice for his maiden, or did some other force lure him with suggestion?

nodes.name: Foot Soldier Cap
nodes.snippet: Flanged iron cap widely issued to foot soldiers in a lord's army.
nodes.lore: Flanged iron cap widely issued to foot soldiers in a lord's army.

nodes.name: Foot Soldier Helm
nodes.snippet: Wholly unembellished iron helm. Widely issued to foot soldiers in a lord's army.
nodes.lore: Wholly unembellished iron helm. Widely issued to foot soldiers in a lord's army.

nodes.name: Gilded Foot Soldier Cap
nodes.snippet: Flanged cap made from gilded iron. Issued to foot soldiers protecting the royal capital of Leyndell.
nodes.lore: Flanged cap made from gilded iron. Issued to foot soldiers protecting the royal capital of Leyndell.

nodes.name: Gelmir Knight Helm
nodes.snippet: Helm worn by knights once loyal to Praetor Rykard. Its crest of red feathers symbolizes Rykard's pedigree as Lord Radagon's son.
nodes.lore: Helm worn by knights once loyal to Praetor Rykard. Its crest of red feathers symbolizes Rykard's pedigree as Lord Radagon's son.

nodes.name: Glintstone Scarab
nodes.snippet: Blue-green scarab worn directly on the head. These scarabs roll clumps of sorcery during their labors.
nodes.lore: Blue-green scarab worn directly on the head. These scarabs roll clumps of sorcery during their labors.

nodes.name: Godrick Knight Helm
nodes.snippet: Gauntlets worn by knights loyal to Godrick the Grafted.
nodes.lore: Gauntlets worn by knights loyal to Godrick the Grafted.

nodes.name: Grass Hair Ornament
nodes.snippet: A headdress made from wildflowers. Wearing this might make you less easy to spot.
nodes.lore: A headdress made from wildflowers. Wearing this might make you less easy to spot.

nodes.name: Godskin Noble Hood
nodes.snippet: Hood made by sewing together patches of smooth skin. Subcutaneous fat makes it plump and soft. Worn by Godskin Nobles. Nobles are the most ancient apostles who are said to have assimilated inhuman physiology. Not unilke the crucible, the Er
nodes.lore: Hood made by sewing together patches of smooth skin. Subcutaneous fat makes it plump and soft. Worn by Godskin Nobles. Nobles are the most ancient apostles who are said to have assimilated inhuman physiology. Not unilke the crucible, the Erdtree in its primordial form.

nodes.name: Foot Soldier Helmet
nodes.snippet: Helmet with a protective metal plate. Widely issued to foot soldiers in lord's army.
nodes.lore: Helmet with a protective metal plate. Widely issued to foot soldiers in lord's army.

nodes.name: Godrick Soldier Helm
nodes.snippet: Helm worn by soldiers loyal to Godrick the Grafted. Made from durable iron, layered with a chainmail coif beneath.
nodes.lore: Helm worn by soldiers loyal to Godrick the Grafted. Made from durable iron, layered with a chainmail coif beneath.

nodes.name: Godskin Apostle Hood
nodes.snippet: Hood made by seweing together patches of smooth skin. Worn by the Godskin Apostles.
nodes.lore: Hood made by seweing together patches of smooth skin. Worn by the Godskin Apostles.

nodes.name: Great Horned Headband
nodes.snippet: Headband decorated by a pair of great horns.
nodes.lore: Headband decorated by a pair of great horns.

nodes.name: Greathood
nodes.snippet: Hood far larger than the head it is meant to cover. A burial shroud of sorts for those who discover, at long last, the truth they sought.
nodes.lore: Hood far larger than the head it is meant to cover. A burial shroud of sorts for those who discover, at long last, the truth they sought.

nodes.name: Guilty Hood
nodes.snippet: Coarse hooded cloak of olive brown. The garb of those accused of lesser crimes, indicated by the collar of sharpened branches.
nodes.lore: Coarse hooded cloak of olive brown. The garb of those accused of lesser crimes, indicated by the collar of sharpened branches.

nodes.name: Haima Glintstone Crown
nodes.snippet: One of the glintstone crowns bestowed upon Raya Lucaria scholars whose pursuits were deemed worthy.
nodes.lore: One of the glintstone crowns bestowed upon Raya Lucaria scholars whose pursuits were deemed worthy.

nodes.name: Guardian Mask
nodes.snippet: Engraved golden mask.
nodes.lore: Engraved golden mask.

nodes.name: Greathelm
nodes.snippet: Sturdy greathelm made from iron. Though it is uncomfortably heavy and obstructs the vision of the wearer, it provides considerable damage negation.
nodes.lore: Sturdy greathelm made from iron. Though it is uncomfortably heavy and obstructs the vision of the wearer, it provides considerable damage negation.

nodes.name: Haligtree Helm
nodes.snippet: Helm worn by soldiers sworn to the Haligtree. An iron helm graced by a crown of unalloyed gold. Increases faith.
nodes.lore: Helm worn by soldiers sworn to the Haligtree. An iron helm graced by a crown of unalloyed gold. Increases faith.

nodes.name: Hierodas Glintstone Crown
nodes.snippet: One of the glintstone crowns bestowed upon Raya Lucaria scholars whose pursuits were deemed worthy. Increases intelligence and endurance to the detriment of FP. The crown of Hierodas was granted to scholars who engaged in nomadic study away
nodes.lore: One of the glintstone crowns bestowed upon Raya Lucaria scholars whose pursuits were deemed worthy. Increases intelligence and endurance to the detriment of FP. The crown of Hierodas was granted to scholars who engaged in nomadic study away from the academy.

nodes.name: Haligtree Knight Helm
nodes.snippet: Helm worn by knights sworn to the Haligtree. Graced by a crown of unalloyed gold.
nodes.lore: Helm worn by knights sworn to the Haligtree. Graced by a crown of unalloyed gold.

nodes.name: High Page Hood
nodes.snippet: Hood of a page selected for special privilege by the noble they serve.
nodes.lore: Hood of a page selected for special privilege by the noble they serve.

nodes.name: Highwayman Hood
nodes.snippet: Hideously stained and ragged hood. Worn by former foot soldiers who have turned to banditry.
nodes.lore: Hideously stained and ragged hood. Worn by former foot soldiers who have turned to banditry.

nodes.name: Imp Head (cat)
nodes.snippet: Head covering made from the largely unaltered head of an impish golem. Resembling a cat, it holds trace amounts of feline intelligence.
nodes.lore: Head covering made from the largely unaltered head of an impish golem. Resembling a cat, it holds trace amounts of feline intelligence.

nodes.name: Hoslow's Helm
nodes.snippet: A twin-tailed silver helm decorated with flowery adornments. Symbol of the head of the head of the revered House of Hoslow.
nodes.lore: A twin-tailed silver helm decorated with flowery adornments. Symbol of the head of the head of the revered House of Hoslow.

nodes.name: Iji's Mirrorhelm
nodes.snippet: Helm fashioned from a crystal looking-glass, said to have never left War Counselor Iji's head. Easily broken and weak against striking attacks.
nodes.lore: Helm fashioned from a crystal looking-glass, said to have never left War Counselor Iji's head. Easily broken and weak against striking attacks.

nodes.name: Imp Head (fanged)
nodes.snippet: Head covering made from the largely unaltered head of an impish golem. Resembling a fanged demon, it holds trace amounts of bestial strength.
nodes.lore: Head covering made from the largely unaltered head of an impish golem. Resembling a fanged demon, it holds trace amounts of bestial strength.

nodes.name: Imp Head (corpse)
nodes.snippet: Head covering made from the largely unaltered head of an impish golem. Resembling the visage of a corpse, it holds trace amounts of lingering faith.
nodes.lore: Head covering made from the largely unaltered head of an impish golem. Resembling the visage of a corpse, it holds trace amounts of lingering faith.

nodes.name: Imp Head (elder)
nodes.snippet: Head covering made from the largely unaltered head of an impish golem. Resembling an elder, it holds trace amounts of arcane knowledge.
nodes.lore: Head covering made from the largely unaltered head of an impish golem. Resembling an elder, it holds trace amounts of arcane knowledge.

nodes.name: Imp Head (long-tongued)
nodes.snippet: Head covering made from the largely unaltered head of an impish golem.
nodes.lore: Head covering made from the largely unaltered head of an impish golem.

nodes.name: Imp Head (wolf)
nodes.snippet: Head covering made from the largely unaltered head of an impish golem. Resembling a wolf, it holds trace amounts of lupine endurance.
nodes.lore: Head covering made from the largely unaltered head of an impish golem. Resembling a wolf, it holds trace amounts of lupine endurance.

nodes.name: Incantation Scarab
nodes.snippet: Golden scarab worn directly on the head. These scarabs roll clumps of incantations during their labors. Slightly reduces the FP costs of incantations, but increases damage taken. As a scarab approaches death, it abandons its rolled treasure
nodes.lore: Golden scarab worn directly on the head. These scarabs roll clumps of incantations during their labors. Slightly reduces the FP costs of incantations, but increases damage taken. As a scarab approaches death, it abandons its rolled treasure and stretches its wings wide for long journey to its home nest.

nodes.name: Iron Kasa
nodes.snippet: Iron kasa of the man who calls himself Shabriri. Crafted to imitate the woven straw hats worn in the Land of Reeds.
nodes.lore: Iron kasa of the man who calls himself Shabriri. Crafted to imitate the woven straw hats worn in the Land of Reeds.

nodes.name: Iron Helmet
nodes.snippet: An iron helmet showing rust on its edges. Cloth covering the mouth and neck provides some resistance to poison and other irritants.
nodes.lore: An iron helmet showing rust on its edges. Cloth covering the mouth and neck provides some resistance to poison and other irritants.

nodes.name: Jar
nodes.snippet: Jar that fits cleanly over the head when upturned. Made with pride by Iron Fist Alexander. In a uniquely jarlike gesture of friendship, it boosts the power of throwing pot items.
nodes.lore: Jar that fits cleanly over the head when upturned. Made with pride by Iron Fist Alexander. In a uniquely jarlike gesture of friendship, it boosts the power of throwing pot items.

nodes.name: Juvenile Scholar Cap
nodes.snippet: Cap worn by young academy scholars, the juveniles birthed anew by the amber egg of Queen Rennala, the head of Raya Lucaria Academy. Yet their rebirth is not without imperfections, and thus do they repeat the process, eventually becoming utt
nodes.lore: Cap worn by young academy scholars, the juveniles birthed anew by the amber egg of Queen Rennala, the head of Raya Lucaria Academy. Yet their rebirth is not without imperfections, and thus do they repeat the process, eventually becoming utterly dependent upon it. Rebirth is as sleep to them, and with each awakening, memory fades into oblivion.

nodes.name: Knight Helm
nodes.snippet: Iron helm of an unknown knight. It is made of thin iron plate. Such metal armor is heavy, but provides considerable damage negation.
nodes.lore: Iron helm of an unknown knight. It is made of thin iron plate. Such metal armor is heavy, but provides considerable damage negation.

nodes.name: Land Of Reeds Helm
nodes.snippet: Helm made from strips of iron fastened together. Worn by warriors of the Land of Reeds. The Land of Reeds has long been locked in a miserable civil war, during which time it has remained alienated from the cultures of its neighbors. Little
nodes.lore: Helm made from strips of iron fastened together. Worn by warriors of the Land of Reeds. The Land of Reeds has long been locked in a miserable civil war, during which time it has remained alienated from the cultures of its neighbors. Little wonder that the entire nation has succumbed to blood-soaked madness, or so it is said.

nodes.name: Kaiden Helm
nodes.snippet: Helm worn by Kaiden sellswords. These hulking, fearless mercenaries, said to hail from the mountaintops of the wintry north, are adept at fighting on horseback. Kaiden is the name of their home settlement.
nodes.lore: Helm worn by Kaiden sellswords. These hulking, fearless mercenaries, said to hail from the mountaintops of the wintry north, are adept at fighting on horseback. Kaiden is the name of their home settlement.

nodes.name: Lazuli Glintstone Crown
nodes.snippet: One of the glintstone crowns bestowed upon Raya Lucaria scholars whose pursuits were deemed worthy.
nodes.lore: One of the glintstone crowns bestowed upon Raya Lucaria scholars whose pursuits were deemed worthy.

nodes.name: Maliketh's Helm
nodes.snippet: Beast's helm made of black iron and decorated with gold. Worn by Maliketh the Black Blade.
nodes.lore: Beast's helm made of black iron and decorated with gold. Worn by Maliketh the Black Blade.

nodes.name: Lusat's Glintstone Crown
nodes.snippet: The giant blue glintstone crown worn by Lusat, primeval current sorcerer.
nodes.lore: The giant blue glintstone crown worn by Lusat, primeval current sorcerer.

nodes.name: Leyndell Knight Helm
nodes.snippet: Helm worn by knights sworn to defend the royal capital of Leyndell.
nodes.lore: Helm worn by knights sworn to defend the royal capital of Leyndell.

nodes.name: Lionel's Helm
nodes.snippet: Round iron helm with a distinctive large brim. Worn by Lionel the Lionhearted.
nodes.lore: Round iron helm with a distinctive large brim. Worn by Lionel the Lionhearted.

nodes.name: Malenia's Winged Helm
nodes.snippet: Winged helm made of unalloyed gold.
nodes.lore: Winged helm made of unalloyed gold.

nodes.name: Malformed Dragon Helm
nodes.snippet: A malformed golden helm. Adorned with various dragon imagery and worn by the misshapen Tree Sentinels.
nodes.lore: A malformed golden helm. Adorned with various dragon imagery and worn by the misshapen Tree Sentinels.

nodes.name: Marais Mask
nodes.snippet: Mask in the image of a white-haired young noble. Customarily worn by the head of House Marais. Increases arcane. The Marais family has a dual history spanning generations, serving as both executioners and castellans of the Shaded Castle. Th
nodes.lore: Mask in the image of a white-haired young noble. Customarily worn by the head of House Marais. Increases arcane.
