nodes.lore: Bizarre catterpillar-shaped beings formed of moss-covered spherical stones and a disturbing eye that looks like that of a human being. They are commonly found as dull groups of seemingly lifeless rocks.

nodes.name: Bladed Talon Eagle
nodes.snippet: The servants of Stormveil come in many shapes, including Eagles that have been enslaved and fitted with blades. Driving them to attack those who approach
nodes.lore: The servants of Stormveil come in many shapes, including Eagles that have been enslaved and fitted with blades. Driving them to attack those who approach

nodes.name: Boar
nodes.snippet: The Boars of Limgrave are less aggressive than normal, but should not be assumed to be passive
nodes.lore: The Boars of Limgrave are less aggressive than normal, but should not be assumed to be passive

nodes.name: Burning Slug
nodes.snippet: Burning Slugs move slowly and are located in the open field.
nodes.lore: Burning Slugs move slowly and are located in the open field.

nodes.name: Crab
nodes.snippet: Crabs thrive in the shallow ponds of Agheel Lake and the shores of Limgrave
nodes.lore: Crabs thrive in the shallow ponds of Agheel Lake and the shores of Limgrave

nodes.name: Construct
nodes.snippet: An Enemy made by Enemies
nodes.lore: An Enemy made by Enemies

nodes.name: Deer
nodes.snippet: Deer roam freely in the plains on Limgrave, now that hunters have all but disappeared
nodes.lore: Deer roam freely in the plains on Limgrave, now that hunters have all but disappeared

nodes.name: Dog
nodes.snippet: Roaming through the fields of Limgrave, wild Dogs should not be assumed to be docile nor dull.
nodes.lore: Roaming through the fields of Limgrave, wild Dogs should not be assumed to be docile nor dull.

nodes.name: Giant Dragonfly
nodes.snippet: The Great Dragonflies of Limgrave buzz and harass all creatures in their vicinity
nodes.lore: The Great Dragonflies of Limgrave buzz and harass all creatures in their vicinity

nodes.name: Direwolf
nodes.snippet: Found alone or in small packs, these bloodthirsty beasts stalk the Lands Between with predatory fervor.
nodes.lore: Found alone or in small packs, these bloodthirsty beasts stalk the Lands Between with predatory fervor.

nodes.name: Eagle
nodes.snippet: Majestic Eagles can be spotted among the cliffs, even during these times.
nodes.lore: Majestic Eagles can be spotted among the cliffs, even during these times.

nodes.name: Giant Crab
nodes.snippet: Giant Crabs crowd the shores and shallows, attacking any who approach by swinging their powerful claws and spewing forth high-pressure water.
nodes.lore: Giant Crabs crowd the shores and shallows, attacking any who approach by swinging their powerful claws and spewing forth high-pressure water.

nodes.name: Giant Land Octopus
nodes.snippet: Giant Land Octopuses are a dangerous foe for any daring to visit the beaches of Limgrave.
nodes.lore: Giant Land Octopuses are a dangerous foe for any daring to visit the beaches of Limgrave.

nodes.name: Giant Lobster
nodes.snippet: The Lands Between are rife with all manner of life forms, naturally evolving over long periods and adapting to the environment. One such creature is the Giant Lobster — a creature that evolved to grow to such enormous sizes in order to bett
nodes.lore: The Lands Between are rife with all manner of life forms, naturally evolving over long periods and adapting to the environment. One such creature is the Giant Lobster — a creature that evolved to grow to such enormous sizes in order to better defend itself against much deadlier threats.

nodes.name: Giant Poison Flower
nodes.snippet: Giant Poison Flowers sprout in unlikely places, and summon down the elements on those who approach them
nodes.lore: Giant Poison Flowers sprout in unlikely places, and summon down the elements on those who approach them

nodes.name: Guillemot
nodes.snippet: The Guillemot on the beaches of Limgrave are docile animals, but they are easily spooked
nodes.lore: The Guillemot on the beaches of Limgrave are docile animals, but they are easily spooked

nodes.name: Lesser Runebear
nodes.snippet: Feral, often massive bears with runic patterns on their chest. Most commonly found scavenging the realms of The Lands Between or fighting other wild creatures.
nodes.lore: Feral, often massive bears with runic patterns on their chest. Most commonly found scavenging the realms of The Lands Between or fighting other wild creatures.

nodes.name: Lesser Red Wolf Of Radagon
nodes.snippet: Hostile, over-sized wolves majestically covered in scarlet fur.
nodes.lore: Hostile, over-sized wolves majestically covered in scarlet fur.

nodes.name: Lesser Spider Hand
nodes.snippet: Lesser Spider Hands are horrific creatures, surpassed only by their more powerful, gigantic counterparts.
nodes.lore: Lesser Spider Hands are horrific creatures, surpassed only by their more powerful, gigantic counterparts.

nodes.name: Land Octopus
nodes.snippet: The peculiar Land Octopus is a rather small but dangerous creature
nodes.lore: The peculiar Land Octopus is a rather small but dangerous creature

nodes.name: Owl
nodes.snippet: Owls can be resting on the cliffs of Stormhill, and would even seem friendly and unconcerned by those passing by
nodes.lore: Owls can be resting on the cliffs of Stormhill, and would even seem friendly and unconcerned by those passing by

nodes.name: Poison Flower
nodes.snippet: Poison Flowers grow near caves and cellars in Limgrave
nodes.lore: Poison Flowers grow near caves and cellars in Limgrave

nodes.name: Land Squirt
nodes.snippet: Land Squirts are repulsive shallow water creatures that emit a poison mist to kill and devour those nearby
nodes.lore: Land Squirts are repulsive shallow water creatures that emit a poison mist to kill and devour those nearby

nodes.name: Rat
nodes.snippet: Giant rats plague the mines and caves of Limgrave, spreading diseases where they go
nodes.lore: Giant rats plague the mines and caves of Limgrave, spreading diseases where they go

nodes.name: Rabbitgaroo
nodes.snippet: A species unique to The Lands Between, as adorable as scared.
nodes.lore: A species unique to The Lands Between, as adorable as scared.

nodes.name: Rotten Stray
nodes.snippet: Undead dogs haunt the depths of Limgrave's mines, attacking all those who dwell into the tunnels
nodes.lore: Undead dogs haunt the depths of Limgrave's mines, attacking all those who dwell into the tunnels

nodes.name: Sheep
nodes.snippet: The sheep from Limgrave have long forgotten captivity, romaing the landscape freely instead.
nodes.lore: The sheep from Limgrave have long forgotten captivity, romaing the landscape freely instead.

nodes.name: Skeletal Slime
nodes.snippet: Skeletal slime drudges the shores and shallows of Limgrave, looking for prey to consume
nodes.lore: Skeletal slime drudges the shores and shallows of Limgrave, looking for prey to consume

nodes.name: Spirit Jellyfish
nodes.snippet: Spirit Jellyfish are passive creatures seeking to protect, unless angered
nodes.lore: Spirit Jellyfish are passive creatures seeking to protect, unless angered

nodes.name: Teardrop Scarab
nodes.snippet: There are both Crimson and Cerulean Teardrop Scarabs. Defeat them to replenish the flasks that match their type.
nodes.lore: There are both Crimson and Cerulean Teardrop Scarabs. Defeat them to replenish the flasks that match their type.

nodes.name: Spider Hand
nodes.snippet: Spider Hands skitter about their environs, often clinging to walls to put the drop on unsuspecting trespassers. They are also capable of burrowing deep beneath the earth, violently bursting out of the ground to grab and crush their prey.
nodes.lore: Spider Hands skitter about their environs, often clinging to walls to put the drop on unsuspecting trespassers. They are also capable of burrowing deep beneath the earth, violently bursting out of the ground to grab and crush their prey.

nodes.name: Wild Mouflon
nodes.snippet: Wild Mouflons become hostile when threatened and fight their aggressors by charging forward and striking them with their large horns.
nodes.lore: Wild Mouflons become hostile when threatened and fight their aggressors by charging forward and striking them with their large horns.

nodes.name: Turtle
nodes.snippet: Turtles are passive creatures almost driven to extinction by those seeking its meat, said to have curative properties
nodes.lore: Turtles are passive creatures almost driven to extinction by those seeking its meat, said to have curative properties

nodes.name: Wolf
nodes.snippet: Wolves hunt the Lands Between in packs, and all those traveling should beware of their dangerous pack leaders
nodes.lore: Wolves hunt the Lands Between in packs, and all those traveling should beware of their dangerous pack leaders

nodes.name: Prawn Miner
nodes.snippet: Hostile over-developed creatures resembling large shrimps. Remarkably agile.
nodes.lore: Hostile over-developed creatures resembling large shrimps. Remarkably agile.

nodes.name: Farum Azula Dragon
nodes.snippet: Farum Azula Dragons fly around the Farum Azula or what's left of it, if any of them notice someone roaming around, they will come to hunt the down. They have the power of thunder and they will use it aggresively towards enemies.
nodes.lore: Farum Azula Dragons fly around the Farum Azula or what's left of it, if any of them notice someone roaming around, they will come to hunt the down. They have the power of thunder and they will use it aggresively towards enemies.

nodes.name: Bloody Finger Nerijus
nodes.snippet: Bloody Finger Nerijus follows the Bloody Finger code, hunting for fellow Tarnished without remorse
nodes.lore: Bloody Finger Nerijus follows the Bloody Finger code, hunting for fellow Tarnished without remorse

nodes.name: Anastasia, Tarnished-eater
nodes.snippet: Invaded by Anastasia, Tarnished-Eater!
nodes.lore: Invaded by Anastasia, Tarnished-Eater!

nodes.name: Edgar The Revenger
nodes.snippet: Edgar the Revenger is a warrior clad in medium armor and wields a halberd. His moveset involves mostly physical attacks and his poise is easily broken with magic.
nodes.lore: Edgar the Revenger is a warrior clad in medium armor and wields a halberd. His moveset involves mostly physical attacks and his poise is easily broken with magic.

nodes.name: Maleigh Marais, Shaded Castle Castellan
nodes.snippet: Maleigh Marais's identity is completely shrouded by their mask and long robes. They are equipped with a rapier and move carefully around opponents. They are easily staggered with magic attacks such as Glintblade Phalanx.
nodes.lore: Maleigh Marais's identity is completely shrouded by their mask and long robes. They are equipped with a rapier and move carefully around opponents. They are easily staggered with magic attacks such as Glintblade Phalanx.

nodes.name: Moongrum Carian Knight
nodes.snippet: Moongrum the Carian Knight is the guardian of Raya Lucaria Academy's Grand Library, he is the last line of defense against intruders who are wanting to slay the head sorcerer of the institute. Just like you, a Tarnished, Moongrum is capable
nodes.lore: Moongrum the Carian Knight is the guardian of Raya Lucaria Academy's Grand Library, he is the last line of defense against intruders who are wanting to slay the head sorcerer of the institute. Just like you, a Tarnished, Moongrum is capable of wielding two different weapons, a shield, can use sorcery, and even use Flasks to recover health. A true warrior of Raya Lucaria.

nodes.name: Recusant Henricus
nodes.snippet: Invaded by Recusant Henricus!
nodes.lore: Invaded by Recusant Henricus!

nodes.name: Albinauric
nodes.snippet: Due to their comically large and round heads, Albinaurics are perpetually hunched-over. They are often found in groups of five or more and they employ rudimentary combat tactics, often attacking recklessly with repeated swings and long comb
nodes.lore: Due to their comically large and round heads, Albinaurics are perpetually hunched-over. They are often found in groups of five or more and they employ rudimentary combat tactics, often attacking recklessly with repeated swings and long combos that leave them open. At times, you may spot an Albinauric performing quick cartwheels on the battlefield, displaying agility despite their stature.

nodes.name: Albinauric Wolfback Archer
nodes.snippet: Guarding the rooftops of Ordina.
nodes.lore: Guarding the rooftops of Ordina.

nodes.name: Banished Knight
nodes.snippet: A tall knight who was banished and bound to serve the Grafted Lord, and to kill those trespassers of Stormveil Castle. They are normally seen wielding a large weapon such as a longsword with a shield or a polearm, and they are fully protect
nodes.lore: A tall knight who was banished and bound to serve the Grafted Lord, and to kill those trespassers of Stormveil Castle. They are normally seen wielding a large weapon such as a longsword with a shield or a polearm, and they are fully protected with a complete set of armor.

nodes.name: Azula Beastman
nodes.snippet: The Beastmen of Farum Azula are ferocious half-man, half-beast warriors. They display remarkable agility and are proficient in a plethora of weaponry including various curved blades, greatswords, shields and spears. Many are also excellent
nodes.lore: The Beastmen of Farum Azula are ferocious half-man, half-beast warriors. They display remarkable agility and are proficient in a plethora of weaponry including various curved blades, greatswords, shields and spears. Many are also excellent marksmen with their unique curved throwing knives which they use with deadly precision.

nodes.name: Baleful Shadow
nodes.snippet: Assassins of the Two Fingers sent forth to carry out their dirty work
nodes.lore: Assassins of the Two Fingers sent forth to carry out their dirty work

nodes.name: Blackflame Monk
nodes.snippet: Mysterious hooded figures belonging to an order of monks that call upon the power of the Black Flame, as well as their astute martial prowess to fell foes and trespassers of their domains.
nodes.lore: Mysterious hooded figures belonging to an order of monks that call upon the power of the Black Flame, as well as their astute martial prowess to fell foes and trespassers of their domains.

nodes.name: Black Knight
nodes.snippet: The Black Knight, riding atop a dark steed, wielding a mighty halberd.
nodes.lore: The Black Knight, riding atop a dark steed, wielding a mighty halberd.

nodes.name: Godrick Knight
nodes.snippet: Wielders of heavy swords and spears, protected by large shields and fully armored. These Knights are often aided in combat by soldiers or hostile dogs, therefore posing a threat to the unprepared and novice.
nodes.lore: Wielders of heavy swords and spears, protected by large shields and fully armored. These Knights are often aided in combat by soldiers or hostile dogs, therefore posing a threat to the unprepared and novice.

nodes.name: Castle Guard
nodes.snippet: Castle Guards comprise the bulk of Stormveil's defenses, but are by no means the most dangerous of the many great ones within.
nodes.lore: Castle Guards comprise the bulk of Stormveil's defenses, but are by no means the most dangerous of the many great ones within.

nodes.name: Chanting Winged Dame
nodes.snippet: The Chanting Winged Dames of Liurnia of The Lakes are equally hostile as they are suspicious.
nodes.lore: The Chanting Winged Dames of Liurnia of The Lakes are equally hostile as they are suspicious.

nodes.name: Commoner
nodes.snippet: The Commoner is a servant that is bound to perform duties for their superiors within Stormveil Castle. They appear to be tall, slender, yet weak, who is still capable of fighting trespassers.
nodes.lore: The Commoner is a servant that is bound to perform duties for their superiors within Stormveil Castle. They appear to be tall, slender, yet weak, who is still capable of fighting trespassers.

nodes.name: Demi-human
nodes.snippet: Agile and hostile, Demi-Humans scavenge the land in groups commanded by a leader, a stronger and more menacing creature. They roam the field light-armored and wielding a wide range of melee weapons and wooden shields.
nodes.lore: Agile and hostile, Demi-Humans scavenge the land in groups commanded by a leader, a stronger and more menacing creature. They roam the field light-armored and wielding a wide range of melee weapons and wooden shields.

nodes.name: Fire Monk
nodes.snippet: Fire Monks hierarchically gather in ceremonies, often at night, burning their way through Liurnia of The Lakes with torches and Mobile Flamethrowers. Fire Monk Abbots have the powerful ability of bursting flames from their hands and must be
nodes.lore: Fire Monks hierarchically gather in ceremonies, often at night, burning their way through Liurnia of The Lakes with torches and Mobile Flamethrowers. Fire Monk Abbots have the powerful ability of bursting flames from their hands and must be fought carefully.

nodes.name: Sorcerer Miner
nodes.snippet: Frail and malnourished, Diggers wield a glintstone-imbued staff for mining which doubles as a weapon when push comes to shove.
nodes.lore: Frail and malnourished, Diggers wield a glintstone-imbued staff for mining which doubles as a weapon when push comes to shove.

nodes.name: Flame Guardian
nodes.snippet: Flame Guardians are slender humanoids dressed in simple white garbs and wearing a bronze mask, concealing their identity. They are equipped with long, flaming poles/staves and wield fire magic.
nodes.lore: Flame Guardians are slender humanoids dressed in simple white garbs and wearing a bronze mask, concealing their identity. They are equipped with long, flaming poles/staves and wield fire magic.

nodes.name: Glintstone Sorcerer
nodes.snippet: Glintstone Sorcerers are decked in Raya Lucarian Robes and distinct Glintstone Crown masks that obscure their identity. These scholars have access to lower level spells such as Glintstone Pebble, among others.
nodes.lore: Glintstone Sorcerers are decked in Raya Lucarian Robes and distinct Glintstone Crown masks that obscure their identity. These scholars have access to lower level spells such as Glintstone Pebble, among others.

nodes.name: Gnoll
nodes.snippet: Hungry Gnolls are usually found eating from dead bodies which makes them vulnerable to surprise or stealth attacks, but they are quick and unforgivingly lethal. They come in different classes, some of the wield a sword and use it very acrob
nodes.lore: Hungry Gnolls are usually found eating from dead bodies which makes them vulnerable to surprise or stealth attacks, but they are quick and unforgivingly lethal. They come in different classes, some of the wield a sword and use it very acrobatically, other use shields and some even are able to cast thunder.

nodes.name: Godrick Soldier
nodes.snippet: The Godrick Soldiers of the Lands Between now roam the landscape, their clothes rags, hoping to rebuild their lives
nodes.lore: The Godrick Soldiers of the Lands Between now roam the landscape, their clothes rags, hoping to rebuild their lives

nodes.name: Golem
nodes.snippet: Golems lay inactive in the hills of Stormhill, but those foolish enough to wake them will soon curse their fate
nodes.lore: Golems lay inactive in the hills of Stormhill, but those foolish enough to wake them will soon curse their fate

nodes.name: Highwayman
nodes.snippet: Former foot soldiers who have turned to banditry. Clandestine and secluded from civilazation, now they lie in hidden caves.
nodes.lore: Former foot soldiers who have turned to banditry. Clandestine and secluded from civilazation, now they lie in hidden caves.

nodes.name: Great Horned Tragoth
nodes.snippet: Invading another world. Defeat Great Horned Tragoth!
nodes.lore: Invading another world. Defeat Great Horned Tragoth!

nodes.name: Gold Dragon Cult Knight
nodes.snippet: Exceedingly tough and skillful Knight, a golden armor-wearing menace lethal to most. Highly trained warrior and a threatening adversary due to its fine combat skills.
nodes.lore: Exceedingly tough and skillful Knight, a golden armor-wearing menace lethal to most. Highly trained warrior and a threatening adversary due to its fine combat skills.

nodes.name: Imp
nodes.snippet: Imps infest many catacombs in the Lands Between, although some theorize they could be protecting their secrets
nodes.lore: Imps infest many catacombs in the Lands Between, although some theorize they could be protecting their secrets

nodes.name: Imp Miner
nodes.snippet: Small and agile creatures that typically conceal themselves within a smokescreen., Often wielding an axe or a flail amd performing flying attacks.
nodes.lore: Small and agile creatures that typically conceal themselves within a smokescreen., Often wielding an axe or a flail amd performing flying attacks.

nodes.name: Invisible Warrior
nodes.snippet: Sneak warriors, patient and quick to get in your back.
nodes.lore: Sneak warriors, patient and quick to get in your back.

nodes.name: Imp
nodes.snippet: Imps infest many catacombs in the Lands Between, although some theorize they could be protecting their secrets
nodes.lore: Imps infest many catacombs in the Lands Between, although some theorize they could be protecting their secrets

nodes.name: Lesser Burial Watchdog
nodes.snippet: A living construct fashioned after felines and hounds, made to watch over catacombs. These constructs wield massive stone weaponry and are attuned to different elements.
nodes.lore: A living construct fashioned after felines and hounds, made to watch over catacombs. These constructs wield massive stone weaponry and are attuned to different elements.

nodes.name: Knight Of Zamor
nodes.snippet: Knights of Zamor are slender humanoids with long hair, wielding a peculiar curved blade. These warriors are graceful and perform almost dance-like maneuvers as they weave across the battlefield. As denizens of the Mountaintops of the Giants
nodes.lore: Knights of Zamor are slender humanoids with long hair, wielding a peculiar curved blade. These warriors are graceful and perform almost dance-like maneuvers as they weave across the battlefield. As denizens of the Mountaintops of the Giants, Knights of Zamor are strongly attuned to Frost, utilizing its powers to bolster their swordplay.

nodes.name: Lesser Pumpkin Head
nodes.snippet: The Lesser Pumpkin Head is a weaker variant of the Pumpkin Head. It appears to be an outcast due to its appearance and is mainly used as one of a weapon to fight against intruders and trespassers alike. The Lesser Pumpkin Head can be seen w
nodes.lore: The Lesser Pumpkin Head is a weaker variant of the Pumpkin Head. It appears to be an outcast due to its appearance and is mainly used as one of a weapon to fight against intruders and trespassers alike. The Lesser Pumpkin Head can be seen wearing a Pumpkin Helm, a distressed cloak, and wields a large club.

nodes.name: Lesser Ulcerated Tree Spirit
nodes.snippet: A guardian that sleeps deep within the crypt of Stormveil Castle. It is quick and very aggressive when it attacks and moves similar to a centipede with its multiple legs. The Lesser Ulcerated Tree Spirit also features two large arms and a t
nodes.lore: A guardian that sleeps deep within the crypt of Stormveil Castle. It is quick and very aggressive when it attacks and moves similar to a centipede with its multiple legs. The Lesser Ulcerated Tree Spirit also features two large arms and a tail that it mainly uses to swing around or to slam the ground.

nodes.name: Living Jar
nodes.snippet: The Living Jar, a walking vase that has long arms used for reaching and attacking their foes. A Living Jar may appear small in a group and it can also appear as a singular giant entity, capable of using slow, yet strong attacks.
nodes.lore: The Living Jar, a walking vase that has long arms used for reaching and attacking their foes. A Living Jar may appear small in a group and it can also appear as a singular giant entity, capable of using slow, yet strong attacks.

nodes.name: Lesser Wormface
nodes.snippet: Lesser Wormface may not be as large as Wormface itself but they can be found roaming around in groups around Crumbling Farum Azula.
nodes.lore: Lesser Wormface may not be as large as Wormface itself but they can be found roaming around in groups around Crumbling Farum Azula.

nodes.name: Maneuverable Flamethrower
nodes.snippet: Large wagons operated from the inside that shoot fire from a sculpture's mouth.
nodes.lore: Large wagons operated from the inside that shoot fire from a sculpture's mouth.

nodes.name: Marionette Gargoyle
nodes.snippet: The Marionette Gargoyle are statues within Raya Lucaria Academy that have been reanimated with magical properties. They mainly fly in the air and wield a scythe that they use to attack their foes. They are usually found on posts, pretending
nodes.lore: The Marionette Gargoyle are statues within Raya Lucaria Academy that have been reanimated with magical properties. They mainly fly in the air and wield a scythe that they use to attack their foes. They are usually found on posts, pretending to be statues.

nodes.name: Marionette Soldier
nodes.snippet: Marionette Soldiers are puppets that are created and given life by superior being that reside within Raya Lucaria Academy. Their purpose is to protect and stop intruders from entering the institute. These soldiers appear to have multiple ar
nodes.lore: Marionette Soldiers are puppets that are created and given life by superior being that reside within Raya Lucaria Academy. Their purpose is to protect and stop intruders from entering the institute. These soldiers appear to have multiple arms wielding more than one weapon, making them unpredictable in regards to how they will strike.

nodes.name: Miner
nodes.snippet: The ever working miners of Limgrave know no life but their toil, and will fiercely defend it
nodes.lore: The ever working miners of Limgrave know no life but their toil, and will fiercely defend it

nodes.name: Mounted Northern Mercenary
nodes.snippet: Mounted Northern Mercenaries are often seen guarding important deliveries and outposts, such as Stormgate
nodes.lore: Mounted Northern Mercenaries are often seen guarding important deliveries and outposts, such as Stormgate

nodes.name: Noble Sorcerer
nodes.snippet: Noble Sorcerers are frail old men wearing tattered garbs and a dirty yellow cape. They use a standard glintstone staff as both a sorcery catalyst and walking stick.
nodes.lore: Noble Sorcerers are frail old men wearing tattered garbs and a dirty yellow cape. They use a standard glintstone staff as both a sorcery catalyst and walking stick.

nodes.name: Misbegotten
nodes.snippet: The Misbegotten are small humanoid creatures with small wings on their backs and a tail that reaches to the ground. Treated as slaves or worse from the time they are born, they rush in attack wildly and ruthlessly with their Iron Cleavers.
nodes.lore: The Misbegotten are small humanoid creatures with small wings on their backs and a tail that reaches to the ground. Treated as slaves or worse from the time they are born, they rush in attack wildly and ruthlessly with their Iron Cleavers.

nodes.name: Kaiden Sellsword
nodes.snippet: Kaiden Sellswords roam the land, looking to be hired for several protection roles
nodes.lore: Kaiden Sellswords roam the land, looking to be hired for several protection roles

nodes.name: Omen
nodes.snippet: A large ogre that is kept imprisoned within the grounds of Stormveil Castle. It appears to be battered and treated violently to discipline it, and used as a weapon to fight against trespassers.
nodes.lore: A large ogre that is kept imprisoned within the grounds of Stormveil Castle. It appears to be battered and treated violently to discipline it, and used as a weapon to fight against trespassers.

nodes.name: Pendulum Statue
nodes.snippet: A golden statue that sits on a small wagon. Attached to it are two pendulum blades that can extend to capture its prey. The Pendulum Statue is being controlled by an unkown creature that rests within it, only by opening its chest will it re
nodes.lore: A golden statue that sits on a small wagon. Attached to it are two pendulum blades that can extend to capture its prey. The Pendulum Statue is being controlled by an unkown creature that rests within it, only by opening its chest will it reveal its root-like tentacles.

nodes.name: Old Knight Istvan
nodes.snippet: Invading another world. Defeat Old Knight Istvan!
nodes.lore: Invading another world. Defeat Old Knight Istvan!

nodes.name: Pumpkin Head
nodes.snippet: The breaking disrupted the flora and fauna of the region. Some took a rather intense liking to the larger plants that emerged
nodes.lore: The breaking disrupted the flora and fauna of the region. Some took a rather intense liking to the larger plants that emerged

nodes.name: Raya Lucaria Sorcerer
nodes.snippet: Academicians of the Raya Lucaria Academy. These sholars dedicate their lives to learning and mastering the art of sorcery and serve one superiror sorcerer who resides within the institute. They are able to use glintstone sorcery to strike t
nodes.lore: Academicians of the Raya Lucaria Academy. These sholars dedicate their lives to learning and mastering the art of sorcery and serve one superiror sorcerer who resides within the institute. They are able to use glintstone sorcery to strike their foes from afar, and are still capable of using their weapon for close-quarters combat.

nodes.name: Rileigh The Idle
nodes.snippet: Invading another world. Defeat Rileigh the Idle!
nodes.lore: Invading another world. Defeat Rileigh the Idle!

nodes.name: Revenant
nodes.snippet: A grotesque amalgamation of limbs, the Revenant is a creature with the body structure of an arachnid and the face of a human. Much like a spider, it possesses many limbs that seem to have been grafted onto its body, taken from diseased huma
nodes.lore: A grotesque amalgamation of limbs, the Revenant is a creature with the body structure of an arachnid and the face of a human. Much like a spider, it possesses many limbs that seem to have been grafted onto its body, taken from diseased humans. The Revenant is capable of traveling through portals it creates with a peculiar form of magic, swiftly emerging behind a foe to catch them off guard.

nodes.name: Servant Of Rot
nodes.snippet: Servants of Rot relish in the pungent miasma expelled by Poison Flowers. Their bodies are heavily mutated by the rot, their heads transformed into large crowns of fungi. These presumably former humans know only the rot and they worship it l
nodes.lore: Servants of Rot relish in the pungent miasma expelled by Poison Flowers. Their bodies are heavily mutated by the rot, their heads transformed into large crowns of fungi. These presumably former humans know only the rot and they worship it like a deity, bathed in its fog of decay.

nodes.name: Raya Lucarian Warden
nodes.snippet: Wardens roam the halls of the Raya Lucaria Academy, armed and watchful for furtive marauders.
nodes.lore: Wardens roam the halls of the Raya Lucaria Academy, armed and watchful for furtive marauders.

nodes.name: Soldier
nodes.snippet: Soldiers defend the many outposts of the Lords from incoming threats, such as the Tarnished
nodes.lore: Soldiers defend the many outposts of the Lords from incoming threats, such as the Tarnished

nodes.name: Skeleton Mage
nodes.snippet: Skeleton Mages are usually found on graveyards in Liurnia of the Lakes. They can out from the ground, they can be found in groups, wielding scythes and constantly casting a spell launching ghostly skulls against you.
nodes.lore: Skeleton Mages are usually found on graveyards in Liurnia of the Lakes. They can out from the ground, they can be found in groups, wielding scythes and constantly casting a spell launching ghostly skulls against you.

nodes.name: Skeleton
nodes.snippet: Skeletons rise from cemeteries and ruins of the Lands Between, attacking relentlessly any who approach
nodes.lore: Skeletons rise from cemeteries and ruins of the Lands Between, attacking relentlessly any who approach

nodes.name: Slender Wayfarer
nodes.snippet: A rare and long-haired creature, tall and slender, often found at the shore.
nodes.lore: A rare and long-haired creature, tall and slender, often found at the shore.

nodes.name: Swordstress
nodes.snippet: Nimble warriors protecting and patrolling the eternal cities.
nodes.lore: Nimble warriors protecting and patrolling the eternal cities.

nodes.name: Troll
nodes.snippet: The Trolls of the Lands Between are frequently enslaved for defensive positions, but are also used in lieu of beasts of burden
nodes.lore: The Trolls of the Lands Between are frequently enslaved for defensive positions, but are also used in lieu of beasts of burden

nodes.name: Troll Knight
nodes.snippet: Trolls are often employed to guard key locations, such as staircases large enough to accommodate their massive bodies. Often unmatched in strength, it bears the question... What manner of creature poses enough of a threat such that the migh
nodes.lore: Trolls are often employed to guard key locations, such as staircases large enough to accommodate their massive bodies. Often unmatched in strength, it bears the question... What manner of creature poses enough of a threat such that the mighty troll would find itself in need of heavy armor...?

nodes.name: Undead Soldier
nodes.snippet: Undead creatures, decayed and struggling to maintain balance while roaming the fields of The Lands Between.
nodes.lore: Undead creatures, decayed and struggling to maintain balance while roaming the fields of The Lands Between.

nodes.name: Twinsage Sorcerer
nodes.snippet: Twinsage Sorcerers are distinguished from other scholars by the crowns worn on their head, depicting the Twinsage Conspectus, with distinct blue crystals forming at the top of the twin heads.
nodes.lore: Twinsage Sorcerers are distinguished from other scholars by the crowns worn on their head, depicting the Twinsage Conspectus, with distinct blue crystals forming at the top of the twin heads.

nodes.name: Wraith Caller
nodes.snippet: As their name suggests, Wraith Callers are equipped with Wraith Calling Bells that they use to channel death magic, summoning vengeful spirits to attack those that dare intrude upon the ruins of Liurnia. Devout worshippers of the Revenants,
nodes.lore: As their name suggests, Wraith Callers are equipped with Wraith Calling Bells that they use to channel death magic, summoning vengeful spirits to attack those that dare intrude upon the ruins of Liurnia. Devout worshippers of the Revenants, Wraith Callers are able to utilize the Revenants' dimensional portals, albeit to a lesser extent.

nodes.name: Bridge Of Sacrifice
nodes.snippet: A busy bridge swarming with iron-clad foes. Be prepared to engage in an array of melee, ranged and artillery attacks or attempt to push past them
nodes.lore: A busy bridge swarming with iron-clad foes. Be prepared to engage in an array of melee, ranged and artillery attacks or attempt to push past them

nodes.name: Church Of Elleh
nodes.snippet: Church of Elleh is a ruined building that hides a Site of Grace. The rooftop has caved in and offers no protection from the elements, and weeds are overtaking the walls, but this doesn't stop merchants from seeking what little shelter is on
nodes.lore: Church of Elleh is a ruined building that hides a Site of Grace. The rooftop has caved in and offers no protection from the elements, and weeds are overtaking the walls, but this doesn't stop merchants from seeking what little shelter is on offer

nodes.name: Deathtouched Catacombs
nodes.snippet: Catacombs filled with the stench of death, Skeletons dwell in the shadows
nodes.lore: Catacombs filled with the stench of death, Skeletons dwell in the shadows

nodes.name: Church Of Dragon Communion
nodes.snippet: The dilapidated remains of the Church of Dragon Communion showcase the ruin that came to the land. But could a flame still linger at its altar??
nodes.lore: The dilapidated remains of the Church of Dragon Communion showcase the ruin that came to the land. But could a flame still linger at its altar??

nodes.name: Coastal Cave
nodes.snippet: Coastal Cave is a dark opening on the side of a cliff. Its mossy depths are overrun by hostile humanoid creatures, but those who brave it may find passage to new lands
nodes.lore: Coastal Cave is a dark opening on the side of a cliff. Its mossy depths are overrun by hostile humanoid creatures, but those who brave it may find passage to new lands

nodes.name: Dragon-burnt Ruins
nodes.snippet: Dragon-Burnt Ruins are what remains of the housing area among Agheel Lake, long lost to the deadly attacks of the fierce Agheel Dragon
nodes.lore: Dragon-Burnt Ruins are what remains of the housing area among Agheel Lake, long lost to the deadly attacks of the fierce Agheel Dragon

nodes.name: Forlorn Hound Evergaol
nodes.snippet: The southern Evergaol of Limgrave, before the entrance to Weeping Peninsula
nodes.lore: The southern Evergaol of Limgrave, before the entrance to Weeping Peninsula

nodes.name: Fringefolk Hero's Grave
nodes.snippet: Fingefolk Hero's Grave is a dark and secluded location within the Cave of Knowledge. The confused and aimless dwell within
nodes.lore: Fingefolk Hero's Grave is a dark and secluded location within the Cave of Knowledge. The confused and aimless dwell within

nodes.name: Fort Haight
nodes.snippet: The overrun fort of Kenneth Haith, the alleged next ruler of Limgrave
nodes.lore: The overrun fort of Kenneth Haith, the alleged next ruler of Limgrave

nodes.name: Divine Tower Of Limgrave
nodes.snippet: Closed with a giant set of doors. It looks like you need some pieces to interact with the tower.
nodes.lore: Closed with a giant set of doors. It looks like you need some pieces to interact with the tower.

nodes.name: Gatefront Ruins
nodes.snippet: Gatefront Ruins is what remains of the now destroyed barracks near the fortification. Despite its poor state, it is a heavily guarded location by those loyal to Stormveil
nodes.lore: Gatefront Ruins is what remains of the now destroyed barracks near the fortification. Despite its poor state, it is a heavily guarded location by those loyal to Stormveil

nodes.name: Limgrave Tunnels
nodes.snippet: Limgrave Tunnels are a mine harvested for Glimstone. Its zealous miners don't dare venture into its hidden chambers, for fear of the hoarder Troll within
nodes.lore: Limgrave Tunnels are a mine harvested for Glimstone. Its zealous miners don't dare venture into its hidden chambers, for fear of the hoarder Troll within

nodes.name: Groveside Cave
nodes.snippet: Groveside Cave is a small refuge for wildlife in Limgrave. Its proximity to water and ease of access make it a sought-after shelter against bad weather, but those who venture in don't seem to return
nodes.lore: Groveside Cave is a small refuge for wildlife in Limgrave. Its proximity to water and ease of access make it a sought-after shelter against bad weather, but those who venture in don't seem to return

nodes.name: Minor Erdtree (mistwood)
nodes.snippet: Easily distiguished from the rest of the trees in Mistwood Forest.
nodes.lore: Easily distiguished from the rest of the trees in Mistwood Forest.

nodes.name: Highroad Cave
nodes.snippet: A cave that houses a tomb, deep within. It is situated at the northmost part of Limgrave. It has been inhabited by a pack of Wolves, Land Octopuses, and Giant Bats
nodes.lore: A cave that houses a tomb, deep within. It is situated at the northmost part of Limgrave. It has been inhabited by a pack of Wolves, Land Octopuses, and Giant Bats

nodes.name: Mistwood
nodes.snippet: Mistwood is a Location in Elden Ring. The Mistwood region is located in the Eastern part of Limgrave. Tiered terrain, small beaches, and luscious forests occupied by Lesser Runebears are the defining features of this region.
nodes.lore: Mistwood is a Location in Elden Ring. The Mistwood region is located in the Eastern part of Limgrave. Tiered terrain, small beaches, and luscious forests occupied by Lesser Runebears are the defining features of this region.

nodes.name: Mistwood Ruins
nodes.snippet: Concealed by the thriving growth of vegetation, these ruins are kept out of sight by the veil of nature itself. But there's more to it than what you see
nodes.lore: Concealed by the thriving growth of vegetation, these ruins are kept out of sight by the veil of nature itself. But there's more to it than what you see

nodes.name: Murkwater Catacombs
nodes.snippet: An underground cemetery that is located at the north-east side of Agheel Lake in Limgrave. Once a place for peaceful rest for fallen heroes, it is now infested by goblins.
nodes.lore: An underground cemetery that is located at the north-east side of Agheel Lake in Limgrave. Once a place for peaceful rest for fallen heroes, it is now infested by goblins.

nodes.name: Murkwater Cave
nodes.snippet: A dark cave halfway through the Murkwater River
nodes.lore: A dark cave halfway through the Murkwater River

nodes.name: Siofra River Well
nodes.snippet: A mystical underground region filled with its own set of skies and stars. Beware, it is teeming with creatures big and small alike
nodes.lore: A mystical underground region filled with its own set of skies and stars. Beware, it is teeming with creatures big and small alike

nodes.name: Smoldering Church
nodes.snippet: The Last Church in Limgrave before reaching Caelid
nodes.lore: The Last Church in Limgrave before reaching Caelid

nodes.name: Stormgate
nodes.snippet: The Stormgate is a heavily guarded area that marks the border within Limgrave and Stormhill. Only the foolhardy would rush such against such odds
nodes.lore: The Stormgate is a heavily guarded area that marks the border within Limgrave and Stormhill. Only the foolhardy would rush such against such odds

nodes.name: Stormfoot Catacombs
nodes.snippet: Stormfoot Catacombs were meant as a restful place for the deceased, but its now overrun by Goblins. Its deepest secrets are secured by the Guardian of the Burial Tree
nodes.lore: Stormfoot Catacombs were meant as a restful place for the deceased, but its now overrun by Goblins. Its deepest secrets are secured by the Guardian of the Burial Tree

nodes.name: Stormhill
nodes.snippet: Stormhill is a Location in Elden Ring. Stormhill is the upper half area of Limgrave, between its central river and Stormveil.
nodes.lore: Stormhill is a Location in Elden Ring. Stormhill is the upper half area of Limgrave, between its central river and Stormveil.

nodes.name: Stormhill Evergaol
nodes.snippet: The Brave and Foolish alike dare venture into the Stormhill Evergaol and face its relentless knight in battle
nodes.lore: The Brave and Foolish alike dare venture into the Stormhill Evergaol and face its relentless knight in battle

nodes.name: Stormhill Shack
nodes.snippet: The Stormhill Shack is a dilapidated shelter formerly used to house hunters in the area. A Site of Grace offers a needed moment of rest for those traveling nearby
nodes.lore: The Stormhill Shack is a dilapidated shelter formerly used to house hunters in the area. A Site of Grace offers a needed moment of rest for those traveling nearby

nodes.name: Stranded Graveyard
nodes.snippet: Stranded Graveyard is a dark and secluded location within the Cave of Knowledge. The confused and aimless dwell within
nodes.lore: Stranded Graveyard is a dark and secluded location within the Cave of Knowledge. The confused and aimless dwell within

nodes.name: Summonwater Village
nodes.snippet: D, Hunter of The Dead, warns you of this nearby village taken over by death, known as Summonwater Village
nodes.lore: D, Hunter of The Dead, warns you of this nearby village taken over by death, known as Summonwater Village

nodes.name: Third Church Of Marika
nodes.snippet: The Ruins of an Old Church found in the Mistwood. The most prominent sight here is a statue of a lady with her arms outstretched
nodes.lore: The Ruins of an Old Church found in the Mistwood. The most prominent sight here is a statue of a lady with her arms outstretched

nodes.name: Warmaster's Shack
nodes.snippet: Small shack in a remote area near north-central Stormhill.
nodes.lore: Small shack in a remote area near north-central Stormhill.

nodes.name: Waypoint Ruins
nodes.snippet: Waypoint Ruins consists of a group of broken buildings and abandoned campsites, now overrun with poisonous flowers and their crazed caretaker
nodes.lore: Waypoint Ruins consists of a group of broken buildings and abandoned campsites, now overrun with poisonous flowers and their crazed caretaker

nodes.name: Ailing Village
nodes.snippet: A small, abandoned village nestled within a forested area north of the Castle Morne Rampart. This village is host to long-dilapidated dwellings, inhabited only by hostile Demi-humans.
nodes.lore: A small, abandoned village nestled within a forested area north of the Castle Morne Rampart. This village is host to long-dilapidated dwellings, inhabited only by hostile Demi-humans.

nodes.name: Bridge Of Sacrifice
nodes.snippet: A busy bridge swarming with iron-clad foes. Be prepared to engage in an array of melee, ranged and artillery attacks or attempt to push past them
nodes.lore: A busy bridge swarming with iron-clad foes. Be prepared to engage in an array of melee, ranged and artillery attacks or attempt to push past them

nodes.name: Callu Baptismal Church
nodes.snippet: A dilapidated church, presumably frequented by the former residents of Ailing Village. Now it lies in ruin, its congregation replaced by a family of mutant vermin feasting on the remains of unfortunate travelers
nodes.lore: A dilapidated church, presumably frequented by the former residents of Ailing Village. Now it lies in ruin, its congregation replaced by a family of mutant vermin feasting on the remains of unfortunate travelers

nodes.name: Church Of Pilgrimage
nodes.snippet: The Church of Pilgrimage is a broken building situated at the top of the cliff in the north of the Weeping Peninsula. Even with the walls and roof destroyed pilgrims still make the journey to visit this church
nodes.lore: The Church of Pilgrimage is a broken building situated at the top of the cliff in the north of the Weeping Peninsula. Even with the walls and roof destroyed pilgrims still make the journey to visit this church

nodes.name: Castle Morne
nodes.snippet: Remants of a chaos swept castle, teeming with iron-clad enemies -now engulfed with fire, piles of remains and ruin.
nodes.lore: Remants of a chaos swept castle, teeming with iron-clad enemies -now engulfed with fire, piles of remains and ruin.

nodes.name: Demi-human Forest Ruins
nodes.snippet: Demi-Humans congregate here to worship their queen. Defeating her will reward players with the Demi-Human Queen's Staff, which is excellent for beginner mages.
nodes.lore: Demi-Humans congregate here to worship their queen. Defeating her will reward players with the Demi-Human Queen's Staff, which is excellent for beginner mages.

nodes.name: Earthbore Cave
nodes.snippet: Earthbore Cave is a small dark cave on the side of the Cliffside between Limgrave and Weeping Peninsula. One might be able to hear the squeaks of rats and the snoring of a large beast deep within if you listen closely
nodes.lore: Earthbore Cave is a small dark cave on the side of the Cliffside between Limgrave and Weeping Peninsula. One might be able to hear the squeaks of rats and the snoring of a large beast deep within if you listen closely

nodes.name: Forest Lookout Tower
nodes.snippet: A run-down tower built on the edge of the cliffs of the Weeping Peninsula, located west of the Bridge of Sacrifice.
nodes.lore: A run-down tower built on the edge of the cliffs of the Weeping Peninsula, located west of the Bridge of Sacrifice.

nodes.name: Impaler's Catacombs
nodes.snippet: The Impaler's Catacombs are found in the east side of the Weeping Peninsula. Once used to store the bodies of the dead, it is now crawling with Imps.
nodes.lore: The Impaler's Catacombs are found in the east side of the Weeping Peninsula. Once used to store the bodies of the dead, it is now crawling with Imps.

nodes.name: Fourth Church Of Marika
nodes.snippet: The ruins of a lonely church, sitting atop a plateau west of the peninsula. Despite its current state, the altar remains intact and ghostly knights still roam the fields nearby, perhaps restless and duty-bound to safeguarding the area...
nodes.lore: The ruins of a lonely church, sitting atop a plateau west of the peninsula. Despite its current state, the altar remains intact and ghostly knights still roam the fields nearby, perhaps restless and duty-bound to safeguarding the area...

nodes.name: Minor Erdtree (weeping Peninsula)
nodes.snippet: One of the large Erdtree's found in the Weeping Peninsula. An Erdtree Avatar charges at those who approach
nodes.lore: One of the large Erdtree's found in the Weeping Peninsula. An Erdtree Avatar charges at those who approach

nodes.name: Isolated Merchant's Shack
nodes.snippet: Found at the western edge of the Weeping Peninsula, this lonesome hut is home to but a single person who has long adapted to a life of seclusion
nodes.lore: Found at the western edge of the Weeping Peninsula, this lonesome hut is home to but a single person who has long adapted to a life of seclusion

nodes.name: Oridys's Rise
nodes.snippet: A lone observation tower atop a plateau in the east of the Weeping Peninsula. Long since abandoned, the entrance is barred by a seal, presenting travelers with a small challenge
nodes.lore: A lone observation tower atop a plateau in the east of the Weeping Peninsula. Long since abandoned, the entrance is barred by a seal, presenting travelers with a small challenge

nodes.name: Morne Tunnel
nodes.snippet: A dark and hidden tunnel that is inhabited by Misbegottens and Miners.
nodes.lore: A dark and hidden tunnel that is inhabited by Misbegottens and Miners.

nodes.name: Tombsward Cave
nodes.snippet: Hidden cave in the forest teeming with poison-filled creatures.
nodes.lore: Hidden cave in the forest teeming with poison-filled creatures.

nodes.name: Tombsward Ruins
nodes.snippet: The ruins of a former burial ground. Next to nothing remains here, yet squires still roam the dark tunnels beneath. Perhaps protecting the graves of their former noble masters..
nodes.lore: The ruins of a former burial ground. Next to nothing remains here, yet squires still roam the dark tunnels beneath. Perhaps protecting the graves of their former noble masters..

nodes.name: Tombsward Catacombs
nodes.snippet: The Tombsward Catacombs are hidden inside the mountain near the Minor Erdtree in the Weeping Peninsula. Once filled with the bodies of the dead, armed skeletons now rise to chase away those that wish to explore its depths.
nodes.lore: The Tombsward Catacombs are hidden inside the mountain near the Minor Erdtree in the Weeping Peninsula. Once filled with the bodies of the dead, armed skeletons now rise to chase away those that wish to explore its depths.

nodes.name: Tower Of Return
nodes.snippet: A dilapidated tower guarded by foot soldiers and mounted cavalry. Makeshift wooden platforms and ladders line the inside of the tower, allowing it to retain some function as a lookout point and a mount for ballistae
nodes.lore: A dilapidated tower guarded by foot soldiers and mounted cavalry. Makeshift wooden platforms and ladders line the inside of the tower, allowing it to retain some function as a lookout point and a mount for ballistae

nodes.name: Weeping Evergaol
nodes.snippet: An evergaol located on the western landmass of the Weeping Peninsula. A formidable opponent is imprisoned within
nodes.lore: An evergaol located on the western landmass of the Weeping Peninsula. A formidable opponent is imprisoned within

nodes.name: Witchbane Ruins
nodes.snippet: A set of ruins found in western Weeping Peninsula. Overgrown with thick shrubs and poisonous flowers, this decrepit landmark invites only misery and dread.
nodes.lore: A set of ruins found in western Weeping Peninsula. Overgrown with thick shrubs and poisonous flowers, this decrepit landmark invites only misery and dread.

nodes.name: Academy Crystal Cave
nodes.snippet: The depths of the Raya Lucaria Academy are rich in rose-tinted crystals, imbued with the natural magic of the area
nodes.lore: The depths of the Raya Lucaria Academy are rich in rose-tinted crystals, imbued with the natural magic of the area

nodes.name: Academy Gate Town
nodes.snippet: The sunken remains of the academy town
nodes.lore: The sunken remains of the academy town

nodes.name: Ainsel River Well
nodes.snippet: A small structure housing a lift that takes you down to the Ainsel River.
nodes.lore: A small structure housing a lift that takes you down to the Ainsel River.

nodes.name: Artist's Shack
nodes.snippet: A small cabin at North Liurnia of The Lakes. Its strongly perceived tranquility seems overshadowed by oblivion.
nodes.lore: A small cabin at North Liurnia of The Lakes. Its strongly perceived tranquility seems overshadowed by oblivion.

nodes.name: Bellum Church
nodes.snippet: Located past the ruins and the forested area guarded by Mounted Knights.
nodes.lore: Located past the ruins and the forested area guarded by Mounted Knights.

nodes.name: Bellum Highway
nodes.snippet: Outside the East Raya Lucaria Gate
nodes.lore: Outside the East Raya Lucaria Gate

nodes.name: Black Knife Catacombs
nodes.snippet: Black Knife Catacombs homes many skeletons and crabs within its depths, find and defeat the Black Knife Assassin within to obtain their dagger.
nodes.lore: Black Knife Catacombs homes many skeletons and crabs within its depths, find and defeat the Black Knife Assassin within to obtain their dagger.

nodes.name: Caria Manor
nodes.snippet: A large estate situated far to the north of Liurnia.
nodes.lore: A large estate situated far to the north of Liurnia.

nodes.name: Boilprawn Shack
nodes.snippet: Small cozy shack where Blackguard Big Boggart resides.
nodes.lore: Small cozy shack where Blackguard Big Boggart resides.

nodes.name: Carian Study Hall
nodes.snippet: A large library-like location full of spectres
nodes.lore: A large library-like location full of spectres

nodes.snippet: A small starting building with trail leading to an area occupied by a gnarly Grafted Scion
nodes.lore: A small starting building with trail leading to an area occupied by a gnarly Grafted Scion

nodes.name: Chelona's Rise
nodes.snippet: A tower overlooking the southern-most tip of Liurnia of the lakes
nodes.lore: A tower overlooking the southern-most tip of Liurnia of the lakes

nodes.snippet: The remnants of an old church in the north of Liurnia, just before the Altus Plateau.
nodes.lore: The remnants of an old church in the north of Liurnia, just before the Altus Plateau.

nodes.name: Cathedral Of Manus Celes
nodes.snippet: A large church atop the southern plateau of Liurnia.
nodes.lore: A large church atop the southern plateau of Liurnia.

nodes.name: Church Of Vows
nodes.snippet: It is here, at the Church of Vows, that the great houses of the Erdtree and the Moon were joined by the matrimonial bond between red-haired Radagon and Rennala of the Full Moon. That is why it holds in view the monuments of both houses, the
nodes.lore: It is here, at the Church of Vows, that the great houses of the Erdtree and the Moon were joined by the matrimonial bond between red-haired Radagon and Rennala of the Full Moon. That is why it holds in view the monuments of both houses, the Erdtree of the Capital and the Academy of Raya Lucaria.

nodes.name: Converted Fringe Tower
nodes.snippet: A tower found on the fringe in the northeastern tip of Liurnia.
nodes.lore: A tower found on the fringe in the northeastern tip of Liurnia.

nodes.name: Converted Tower
nodes.snippet: An abandoned tower
nodes.lore: An abandoned tower

nodes.name: Cuckoo's Evergaol
nodes.snippet: Evergaol located on the western landmass of Liurnia. A powerful force stirs within.
nodes.lore: Evergaol located on the western landmass of Liurnia. A powerful force stirs within.

nodes.name: Deep Ainsel Well
nodes.snippet: A deep well leading to the Moonlight Altar from the underground Grand Cloister.
nodes.lore: A deep well leading to the Moonlight Altar from the underground Grand Cloister.

nodes.name: Divine Tower Of Liurnia
nodes.snippet: Divine Tower of Liurnia is a Location in Elden Ring. The Divine Tower of Liurnia is found in Liurnia of the Lakes and is reached by taking the lift down at the secret area of Carian Study Hall.
nodes.lore: Divine Tower of Liurnia is a Location in Elden Ring. The Divine Tower of Liurnia is found in Liurnia of the Lakes and is reached by taking the lift down at the secret area of Carian Study Hall.

nodes.snippet: A village in Liurnia gripped in the madness of Three Fingers. Its denizens know nothing but the fiery frenzy burning within them
nodes.lore: A village in Liurnia gripped in the madness of Three Fingers. Its denizens know nothing but the fiery frenzy burning within them

nodes.name: Grand Lift Of Dectus
nodes.snippet: A massive lift platform found between Liurnia and the Altus Plateau. The mechanism that operates the lift is barred by four Guardian Statues, requiring the brandishing of a special artifact for them to release their hold
nodes.lore: A massive lift platform found between Liurnia and the Altus Plateau. The mechanism that operates the lift is barred by four Guardian Statues, requiring the brandishing of a special artifact for them to release their hold

nodes.lore: A lone tower overlooking the Liurnia main road on the eastern landmass

nodes.snippet: Jarburg was a peaceful village, but the effects of the Shattering affected even the otherwise amicable Living Jar
nodes.lore: Jarburg was a peaceful village, but the effects of the Shattering affected even the otherwise amicable Living Jar

nodes.name: Kingsrealm Ruins
nodes.snippet: A ruined area, only seemingly bare.
nodes.lore: A ruined area, only seemingly bare.

nodes.snippet: Hidden on the southwestern cliffs of Liurnia of the Lakes, a winding cave carved by large crystals
nodes.lore: Hidden on the southwestern cliffs of Liurnia of the Lakes, a winding cave carved by large crystals

nodes.snippet: Ruins of a sunken structure beset by fog, found amidst the marshes of south Liurnia.
nodes.lore: Ruins of a sunken structure beset by fog, found amidst the marshes of south Liurnia.

nodes.snippet: The ruins of a large estate found atop the southwestern plateau of Liurnia.
nodes.lore: The ruins of a large estate found atop the southwestern plateau of Liurnia.

nodes.snippet: An evergaol overlooking the vast lakes. An ever burning flame resides within

nodes.snippet: Ruins found in the southwest of the Liurnia of the Lakes region
nodes.lore: Ruins found in the southwest of the Liurnia of the Lakes region

nodes.snippet: The holy remains now lie in ruin
nodes.lore: The holy remains now lie in ruin

nodes.snippet: A tower beset by large chunks of glintstone emerging from the ground and penetrating its interiors. Home to a mysterious figure observing The Lands Between
nodes.lore: A tower beset by large chunks of glintstone emerging from the ground and penetrating its interiors. Home to a mysterious figure observing The Lands Between

nodes.lore: One of the vast networks of tunnels used prominently for the academy's crystal mining operation.

nodes.name: Renna's Rise
nodes.snippet: A Tower of the Three Sisters, belonging to Renna. It houses Ranni's Snow Witch Armor Set
nodes.lore: A Tower of the Three Sisters, belonging to Renna. It houses Ranni's Snow Witch Armor Set

nodes.snippet: An isolated shack north of the Minor Erdtree. Bodies litter the ground inside the shack, possibly belonging to those one has taken revenge upon
nodes.lore: An isolated shack north of the Minor Erdtree. Bodies litter the ground inside the shack, possibly belonging to those one has taken revenge upon

nodes.snippet: An evergaol where a nortorious assassin is incarcerated
nodes.lore: An evergaol where a nortorious assassin is incarcerated

nodes.snippet: Catacombs at the end of the world..
nodes.lore: Catacombs at the end of the world..

nodes.name: Rose Church
nodes.snippet: The ruins of a former church aptly named Rose Church, for its walls are now festering with scarlet rot and overgrown with bloodroses.
nodes.lore: The ruins of a former church aptly named Rose Church, for its walls are now festering with scarlet rot and overgrown with bloodroses.

nodes.snippet: An evergaol constructed for the Carian Royal family in the plains east of the royal estate
nodes.lore: An evergaol constructed for the Carian Royal family in the plains east of the royal estate

nodes.snippet: A steep precipice with abundant caves in the insides all the way through
nodes.lore: A steep precipice with abundant caves in the insides all the way through

nodes.name: Seluvis's Rise
nodes.snippet: A tower bequeathed unto Preceptor Seluvis. Serves as his personal study as he delves into the sorcerous arts.
nodes.lore: A tower bequeathed unto Preceptor Seluvis. Serves as his personal study as he delves into the sorcerous arts.

nodes.name: Slumbering Wolf's Shack
nodes.snippet: A small shack nestled at the side of a cliff, only accessible through a long winding cave system. A woman and her wolf companion rest here.

nodes.name: Aeonia Swamp
nodes.snippet: The Swamp of Aeonia is nestled in the center of the Caelid region. What were once flourishing plains filled with rich flora and fauna are now reduced to a cesspool of death and decay. A high concentration of the dreaded scarlet rot has muta
nodes.lore: The Swamp of Aeonia is nestled in the center of the Caelid region. What were once flourishing plains filled with rich flora and fauna are now reduced to a cesspool of death and decay. A high concentration of the dreaded scarlet rot has mutated the very earth itself, twisting the creatures and plant life that once called this home.

nodes.lore: Capital Outskirts is a Location in Elden Ring. The Capital Outskirts sub-region lies in the Eastern area of the Altus Plateau and is the region just outside Leyndell, Royal Capital but still within the capital's outer walls. Beware of Skeletons rising from their graves and rocks flying out of the capital toward the battlegrounds when exploring the area.

nodes.lore: Leyndell, Royal Capital is a Location in Elden Ring. The Leyndell, Royal Capital region is only accessible to players who have gathered two Great Runes or by following Ranni the Witch quest line and defeating Fia's Champions near the Great Waterfall Crest Site of Grace in the Deeproot Depths, and has content for highly leveled gameplay. For a detailed walkthrough, please visit the walkthrough page for Leyndell, Royal Capital Legacy Dungeon here.

meta.title: Realm of Shadow — Shadow of the Erdtree
meta.note: Curated, lore-accurate dataset (not from the API).

nodes.name: Miquella the Kind
nodes.motive: Shed his flesh, his love, his fear and his doubt to be reborn a benevolent god and raise an Age of Compassion — yet his 'kindness' charms all who behold him into helpless devotion.
nodes.lore: The empyrean twin of Malenia. Climbing the great tower of Enir-Ilim, Miquella abandons the Golden Order and casts away pieces of himself to ascend.

nodes.name: Promised Consort Radahn
nodes.motive: Bound by an oath sworn in childhood to become Miquella's consort and Elden Lord — the martial throne upon which the new god's order would stand.
nodes.lore: Starscourge Radahn, returned as Miquella's promised consort, his soul housed in the body of Mohg.

nodes.name: St. Trina
nodes.motive: Miquella's discarded love, given her own will. Alone among his works she would STOP him — offering an eternal sleep to end his ascent before its cost is paid.
nodes.lore: The cast-off other self of Miquella; a voice of sleep and of mercy turned against its maker.

nodes.name: Thiollier
nodes.motive: A devotee of St. Trina who seeks her doctrine of sleep and death, doubting the bright promise of Miquella.
nodes.lore: A pale aristocrat-turned-disciple who follows Trina's whispered path.

nodes.name: Leda, Needle Knight
nodes.motive: Self-appointed enforcer of Miquella's faithful — she will purge any companion she deems a threat to her lord's design.
nodes.lore: A knight who gathers Miquella's followers, then turns her blade on them in his name.

nodes.name: Malenia, Blade of Miquella
nodes.motive: Sworn shield to her twin Miquella; her undefeated blade and her bloom of scarlet rot are the price of that devotion.
nodes.lore: Miquella's twin, the Severed; her rot scoured Caelid in her duel with Radahn.

nodes.name: Romina, Saint of the Bud
nodes.motive: Guardian of the rot-choked path to Miquella's tower; a saint who embraced scarlet rot as devotion.
nodes.lore: Saint of the Bud, barring the gate of divine beasts on the road to Enir-Ilim.

nodes.name: Enir-Ilim
nodes.motive: The Hornsent's great tower reaching for the heavens — the stage of Miquella's godhood.
nodes.lore: The spiraling tower-city where one might 'stand before the gate' and become divine.

nodes.name: Mohg, Lord of Blood
nodes.motive: Abducted Miquella's cocoon to raise a Mohgwyn dynasty of blood — unwittingly preparing the vessel Miquella needed.
nodes.lore: Lord of Blood, whose corpse becomes the body of the Promised Consort.

nodes.name: Sir Ansbach
nodes.motive: A loyal servant of Mohg's pure-blood dynasty who follows Miquella's trail to weigh — and perhaps avenge — his fallen lord.
nodes.lore: Pureblood Knight of the Mohgwyn dynasty, courteous even in ruin.

nodes.name: Midra, Lord of Frenzied Flame
nodes.motive: A gentle scholar driven to despair until the Frenzied Flame took root — the yearning to burn all life and thought to ash.
nodes.lore: Host of the Frenzied Flame within the Manus Celes library of the Shadow Keep.

nodes.name: Messmer the Impaler
nodes.motive: Marika's abandoned son, gifted a sealed abyssal serpent-flame and commanded to lead a crusade that burned the Hornsent — a war his mother erased from memory.
nodes.lore: The Impaler, his flame sealed behind a divine seal, ruler of the Shadow Keep.

nodes.name: Rellana, Twin Moon Knight
nodes.motive: Messmer's elder sister who left for Liurnia and mastered Carian moon sorcery, swearing her swords to a love beyond the crusade.
nodes.lore: Twin Moon Knight of Castle Ensis, wielder of dark and light moon blades.

nodes.name: Commander Gaius
nodes.motive: A boar-riding commander of Messmer's host, raised among the war-orphans, sworn to the crusade.
nodes.lore: Commander of Messmer's army, guarding the approach to the Shadow Keep.

nodes.name: Divine Beast Dancing Lion
nodes.motive: A storm-wreathed divine beast bound in service, guarding the rites of Belurat.
nodes.lore: A two-souled divine beast of wind, frost and lightning.

nodes.name: Queen Marika
nodes.motive: A Hornsent shaman who, after her people's slaughter, ascended to godhood and took terrible vengeance — sealing the shadow realm and its sins out of history.
nodes.lore: The Eternal Queen; her ascent and her crusade are the wound the DLC reopens.

nodes.name: The Hornsent
nodes.motive: The horned people who once ruled the shadow land and made saints by sealing the living in jars; persecuted and erased, they cry for justice — or for vengeance.
nodes.lore: Once the dominant civilization of the Land of Shadow, undone by Marika's crusade.

nodes.name: The Shadow Tree (Scadutree)
nodes.motive: The shadow cast by the Erdtree; the source of grace in the Land of Shadow, around which the realm's faith turns.
nodes.lore: The Scadutree, whose blessing strengthens those who walk the realm of shadow.

nodes.name: Count Ymir, Mother of Fingers
nodes.motive: A sage who would murder the messengers of the Greater Will and birth a NEW god and new Fingers of his own making.
nodes.lore: Master Sage of the Church of the Bud, scheming to supplant the outer god's guidance.

nodes.name: Metyr, Mother of Fingers
nodes.motive: The Greater Will's severed messenger and mother of the Two/Three Fingers — abandoned by her god, she signals into a silent void.
nodes.lore: An ancient star-born vessel, origin of the Fingers that relayed the Greater Will.

nodes.name: The Greater Will
nodes.motive: The distant outer god whose order shaped the Lands Between — and who long ago fell silent, abandoning its own messenger.
nodes.lore: The outer god behind the Golden Order; its absence drives Ymir and Metyr's grief.

nodes.name: Bayle the Dread
nodes.motive: An ancient dragon who turned on his own kind out of dread of the Greater Will — fear made into a tyrant of storms and rot.
nodes.lore: The Dread, scarred foe of Placidusax's line, hunted through the Jagged Peak.

nodes.name: Dragon Priestess Florissax
nodes.motive: Last loyal drake-priestess who would restore the dragons' glory and see Bayle's dread undone.
nodes.lore: Florissax, keeper of dragon communion and ally against Bayle.
